          ((PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom U.ι).hom
            (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom j).hom).hom.app P) := by

  exact sheafificationCompPullback_comp j U.ι P

nodes.statement:
ok
[Cone B / c.1: object-identification iso \((\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}} \cong (M|_f)_{\mathrm{val}}\)]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(f : Y \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion and \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), and let
  \(\beta\) be the whiskered direct-image natural isomorphism whose pushforward is
  left-adjoint-equivalent to \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) --- the data fixed inside
  \cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison}. There is a canonical isomorphism of presheaves of modules
  over \(Y\)
  \[
    (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}
      \;\cong\; \bigl(M|_f\bigr)_{\mathrm{val}},
  \]
  identifying the direct image of the underlying presheaf \(M_{\mathrm{val}}\) with the underlying
  presheaf of the restricted module \(M|_f = \mathtt{restrict}\,f\,M\). It is the foundational
  object-level identification through which the pushforward presentation of \(\theta\) is reconciled with
  the restriction-world form consumed downstream.
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison, def:restrictfunctorisopullback, lem:sheafification_comp_pullback_eq_leftadjointuniq}
  \leanok
  Compose two canonical identifications. First, the adjunction-uniqueness isomorphism built inside
  \cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison} re-presents the abstract presheaf pullback
  \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) as the pushforward \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\) on objects, giving
  \((\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}} \cong
  (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\). Second, the \(\mathrm{val}\)-component of
  the restriction--pullback comparison \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\,f\)
  (\cref{def:restrictfunctorisopullback}) followed by the sheafify-then-pullback identification
  (\cref{lem:sheafification_comp_pullback_eq_leftadjointuniq}) relates
  \((\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\) to \((M|_f)_{\mathrm{val}}\). Both
  ingredients are canonical isomorphisms already in hand; their composite is the asserted iso, and
  poset-thinness of \(\mathrm{Opens}\,Y\) makes the naturality square of the assembled family automatic.

nodes.title: The presheaf pullback pseudofunctoriality isomorphism \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\)
nodes.statement:
\textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  For composable ring-presheaf maps \(\varphi, \psi\), the composition of the two presheaf pullback
  functors is canonically isomorphic to the pullback along the composite:
  \[
    \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi \,\circ\, \mathtt{pullback}\,\psi
      \;\cong\; \mathtt{pullback}\,(\varphi \mathbin{;} \psi).
  \]
  By construction it \emph{is} the left-adjoint composition isomorphism
  \(\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\) of the three pullback--pushforward adjunctions, reconciled by the
  pushforward composition \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\); this definitional identity is the content of
  \cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_pullbackcomp_inv}. It is leg~1 of the cocycle
  \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_restrict}.

nodes.title: Restriction along a composite as a composition of restrictions \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\)
nodes.statement:
\textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  For composable open immersions \(h : Z \hookrightarrow Y\) and \(f : Y \hookrightarrow X\), restriction
  of sheaves of modules along the composite is canonically isomorphic to the composition of the two
  restrictions:
  \[
    \mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,(h \mathbin{;} f)
      \;\cong\; \mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,f \,\circ\, \mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,h .
  \]
  Its contravariant image under the dual --- \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\)
  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}) of the forgetful image of
  \((\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,h\,f).\mathrm{app}\,M\) --- is the dual-target pseudofunctoriality
  leg~4 of the cocycle \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_restrict}.

ok
[Mate of a \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) comparison is the identity]
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(F, F' : C_0 \to C_1\) be two left adjoints of one and the same right adjoint
  \(G : C_1 \to C_0\), via \(\mathrm{adj}_1 : F \dashv G\) and \(\mathrm{adj}_2 : F' \dashv G\). Then the
  mate (conjugate) of the comparison isomorphism \((\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\,\mathrm{adj}_1\,\mathrm{adj}_2).\mathrm{hom}\)
  under the conjugation bijection \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv}\,\mathrm{adj}_2\,\mathrm{adj}_1\) is the identity
  natural transformation \(\mathbf{1}_G\). This is the abstract content behind every
  \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) cocycle: the comparison transports the unit of one adjunction to the
  other and is therefore mate to \(\mathbf{1}_G\).
nodes.proof_tex:
\leanok
  Unfold \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) as the symmetrisation of the conjugate-iso bijection and apply the
  Mathlib computation \(\mathtt{conjugateIsoEquiv\_symm\_apply\_inv}\) followed by the
  \(\mathtt{Equiv.apply\_symm\_apply}\) round-trip, which collapses the mate to \(\mathbf{1}_G\).
nodes.lean_source:
lemma conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointUniq_hom {F F' : C₀ ⥤ C₁} {G : C₁ ⥤ C₀}
    (adj1 : F ⊣ G) (adj2 : F' ⊣ G) :
    conjugateEquiv adj2 adj1 (leftAdjointUniq adj1 adj2).hom = 𝟙 G := by
  rw [leftAdjointUniq, Iso.symm_hom, conjugateIsoEquiv_symm_apply_inv, Iso.refl_inv,
    Equiv.apply_symm_apply]

nodes.statement:
ok
[Mate of the \(\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\) hom is the reconciling inverse]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Given three adjunctions \(\mathrm{adj}_{01} : F_{01} \dashv G_{10}\),
  \(\mathrm{adj}_{12} : F_{12} \dashv G_{21}\), \(\mathrm{adj}_{02} : F_{02} \dashv G_{20}\) and a
  right-adjoint composition isomorphism \(e : G_{21} \circ G_{10} \cong G_{20}\), the mate (conjugate)
  of \((\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\,\mathrm{adj}_{01}\,\mathrm{adj}_{12}\,\mathrm{adj}_{02}\,e).\mathrm{hom}\)
  under \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv}\,\mathrm{adj}_{02}\,(\mathrm{adj}_{01}.\mathrm{comp}\,\mathrm{adj}_{12})\)
  equals \(e.\mathrm{inv}\). This is the companion of Mathlib's
  \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_leftAdjointCompIso\_inv}\) (which computes the conjugate of the \(\mathrm{inv}\)).
nodes.proof_tex:
\leanok
  By \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_leftAdjointCompIso\_inv}\) the conjugate of the \(\mathrm{inv}\) is
  \(e.\mathrm{hom}\); rewriting \(e.\mathrm{hom}\) as that conjugate and using functoriality of the
  conjugation bijection under composition (\(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_comp}\)) together with
  \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_id}\) shows the conjugate of the \(\mathrm{hom}\) post-composed with
  \(e.\mathrm{hom}\) is the identity. Cancelling the mono \(e.\mathrm{hom}\) identifies the conjugate
  with \(e.\mathrm{inv}\).
nodes.lean_source:
lemma conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_hom
    (adj₀₁ : F₀₁ ⊣ G₁₀) (adj₁₂ : F₁₂ ⊣ G₂₁) (adj₀₂ : F₀₂ ⊣ G₂₀) (e : G₂₁ ⋙ G₁₀ ≅ G₂₀) :
    conjugateEquiv adj₀₂ (adj₀₁.comp adj₁₂)
        (leftAdjointCompIso adj₀₁ adj₁₂ adj₀₂ e).hom = e.inv := by
  have hcomp : conjugateEquiv adj₀₂ (adj₀₁.comp adj₁₂)
        (leftAdjointCompIso adj₀₁ adj₁₂ adj₀₂ e).hom ≫ e.hom = 𝟙 _ := by
    conv_lhs => rw [show e.hom = conjugateEquiv (adj₀₁.comp adj₁₂) adj₀₂
      (leftAdjointCompIso adj₀₁ adj₁₂ adj₀₂ e).inv from
        (conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_inv adj₀₁ adj₁₂ adj₀₂ e).symm]
    rw [conjugateEquiv_comp, Iso.inv_hom_id, conjugateEquiv_id]
  rw [← cancel_mono e.hom, hcomp, e.inv_hom_id]

nodes.title: Explicit component of the \(\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\) hom
nodes.statement:
\textit{Provided by Mathlib (\texttt{Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Adjunction/CompositionIso.lean}).}
  Given three adjunctions \(\mathrm{adj}_{01} : F_{01} \dashv G_{10}\),
  \(\mathrm{adj}_{12} : F_{12} \dashv G_{21}\), \(\mathrm{adj}_{02} : F_{02} \dashv G_{20}\) and a
  right-adjoint composition isomorphism \(e : G_{21} \circ G_{10} \cong G_{20}\), the hom of the
  left-adjoint composition isomorphism is \emph{fully explicit} at each object \(X\):
  \[
    (\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\,\mathrm{adj}_{01}\,\mathrm{adj}_{12}\,\mathrm{adj}_{02}\,e).\mathrm{hom}.\mathrm{app}\,X
      \;=\; F_{12}.\mathrm{map}\bigl(F_{01}.\mathrm{map}\,(\mathrm{adj}_{02}.\mathrm{unit}.\mathrm{app}\,X)\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; F_{12}.\mathrm{map}\bigl(F_{01}.\mathrm{map}\,(e.\mathrm{inv}.\mathrm{app}\,\cdot)\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; F_{12}.\mathrm{map}\,(\mathrm{adj}_{01}.\mathrm{counit}.\mathrm{app}\,\cdot)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathrm{adj}_{12}.\mathrm{counit}.\mathrm{app}\,\cdot,
  \]
  i.e. the unit of \(\mathrm{adj}_{02}\) pushed through \(F_{01}\) and \(F_{12}\), the reconciling
  \(e.\mathrm{inv}\), and the two counits of \(\mathrm{adj}_{01}\) and \(\mathrm{adj}_{12}\). When the
  reconciling iso is the identity (\(e = \mathtt{Iso.refl}\)) the middle \(e.\mathrm{inv}\) factor drops.
  This is the section-level component companion of the mate characterisation
  \cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointcompiso_hom}.

ok
[Abstract \(H1\) cocycle: \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) intertwines two \(\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\)s]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(F_\bullet\) and \(P_\bullet\) be two families of left adjoints sharing the right adjoints
  \(G_{\bullet\bullet}\) level by level: \(\mathrm{adjF}_{ij} : F_{ij} \dashv G_{ji}\) and
  \(\mathrm{adjP}_{ij} : P_{ij} \dashv G_{ji}\) for \(ij \in \{01, 12, 02\}\), together with a single
  shared right-adjoint composition isomorphism \(e : G_{21} \circ G_{10} \cong G_{20}\). Write
  \(H1_{ij} = \mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\,\mathrm{adjF}_{ij}\,\mathrm{adjP}_{ij} : F_{ij} \cong P_{ij}\)
  for the comparison isomorphisms and \(FC, PC\) for the two
  \(\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\)s built from the \emph{same} \(e\). Then the \(H1\)s intertwine the two
  composition isomorphisms:
  \[
    FC.\mathrm{hom} \;\mathbin{;}\; (H1_{02}).\mathrm{hom}
      \;=\; \bigl((H1_{01}).\mathrm{hom} \,\triangleright\, F_{12}\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \bigl(P_{01} \,\triangleleft\, (H1_{12}).\mathrm{hom}\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; PC.\mathrm{hom} .
  \]
  This is the abstract \(H1\) cocycle instantiated in the Cone~B/c.2 lemma
  \cref{lem:presheafdual_h1_cocycle}, and the generic engine behind
  \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_restrictFunctorComp\_inv}\)
  (\cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_restrictfunctorcomp_inv}): both reduce a composite comparison to a chain
  over the two composition isomorphisms.
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointuniq_hom, lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointcompiso_hom}
  \leanok
  Apply the conjugation bijection \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv}\), which is injective, to both sides. On the
  left, functoriality under composition turns the mate of \(FC.\mathrm{hom} \mathbin{;} (H1_{02}).\mathrm{hom}\)
  into the mate of \(H1_{02}\) composed with the mate of \(FC.\mathrm{hom}\); by
  \cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointuniq_hom} the first is \(\mathbf{1}\) and by
  \cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointcompiso_hom} the second is \(e.\mathrm{inv}\), so the left mate is
  \(e.\mathrm{inv}\). On the right, the threefold composite splits under
  \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_comp}\); the two whiskered \(H1\) factors collapse via
  \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_whiskerRight}\) and \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_whiskerLeft}\) to identities by
  \cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointuniq_hom}, and the \(PC.\mathrm{hom}\) factor becomes
  \(e.\mathrm{inv}\) by \cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointcompiso_hom}. Both mates equal \(e.\mathrm{inv}\),
  so the two morphisms agree.
nodes.lean_source:
lemma leftAdjointUniq_leftAdjointCompIso_comm
    {P₀₁ : C₀ ⥤ C₁} {P₁₂ : C₁ ⥤ C₂} {P₀₂ : C₀ ⥤ C₂}
    (adjF01 : F₀₁ ⊣ G₁₀) (adjF12 : F₁₂ ⊣ G₂₁) (adjF02 : F₀₂ ⊣ G₂₀)
    (adjP01 : P₀₁ ⊣ G₁₀) (adjP12 : P₁₂ ⊣ G₂₁) (adjP02 : P₀₂ ⊣ G₂₀)
    (e : G₂₁ ⋙ G₁₀ ≅ G₂₀) :
    (leftAdjointCompIso adjF01 adjF12 adjF02 e).hom ≫ (leftAdjointUniq adjF02 adjP02).hom =
      Functor.whiskerRight (leftAdjointUniq adjF01 adjP01).hom F₁₂ ≫
        Functor.whiskerLeft P₀₁ (leftAdjointUniq adjF12 adjP12).hom ≫
        (leftAdjointCompIso adjP01 adjP12 adjP02 e).hom := by
  apply (conjugateEquiv adjP02 (adjF01.comp adjF12)).injective

  rw [← conjugateEquiv_comp adjP02 adjF02 (adjF01.comp adjF12),
    conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointUniq_hom adjF02 adjP02,
    conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_hom, Category.id_comp]

  rw [← conjugateEquiv_comp adjP02 (adjP01.comp adjF12) (adjF01.comp adjF12)
        (Functor.whiskerLeft P₀₁ (leftAdjointUniq adjF12 adjP12).hom ≫
          (leftAdjointCompIso adjP01 adjP12 adjP02 e).hom)
        (Functor.whiskerRight (leftAdjointUniq adjF01 adjP01).hom F₁₂),
    ← conjugateEquiv_comp adjP02 (adjP01.comp adjP12) (adjP01.comp adjF12)
        (leftAdjointCompIso adjP01 adjP12 adjP02 e).hom
        (Functor.whiskerLeft P₀₁ (leftAdjointUniq adjF12 adjP12).hom),
    conjugateEquiv_whiskerRight adjP01 adjF01 adjF12,
    conjugateEquiv_whiskerLeft adjP12 adjF12 adjP01,
    conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointUniq_hom adjF01 adjP01,
    conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointUniq_hom adjF12 adjP12,
    conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_hom]
  simp

nodes.title: Cone B / c.2(a): cocycle law of the adjunction-uniqueness iso \(H1\) over the immersion factorisation
nodes.statement:
ok
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(h : Z \hookrightarrow Y\) and \(f : Y \hookrightarrow X\) be composable open immersions, write
  \(\varphi_g = g.\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom}.\mathrm{hom}\), and let
  \[
    H1_g \;=\; (\mathtt{pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\,\cdots).\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\,
      (\mathtt{pullbackPushforwardAdjunction}\,\varphi_g)
      \;\colon\; \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_g \;\cong\; \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi_g
  \]
  be the left-adjoint-uniqueness isomorphism re-presenting the presheaf pullback as the pushforward
  (Stage~H1 of \cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison}). Then \(H1\) satisfies a \emph{cocycle}
  (composition) law over the factorisation \(h \mathbin{;} f\): on the pullback (source) flank it is
  threaded by the presheaf pseudofunctoriality iso \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,\varphi_f\,\varphi_h\)
  (\cref{lem:presheafofmodules_pullbackcomp}) and on the pushforward (target) flank by the
  restriction-composition iso \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,h\,f\)
  (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_restrictfunctorcomp}, equivalently the pushforward composition
  \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\)). Writing \(FC = \mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\) for the left-adjoint
  composition isomorphism of the three pushforward adjunctions
  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_g \dashv \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi_g\), reconciled by the presheaf
  pushforward composition \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\,\varphi_f\,\varphi_h\), the realized cocycle is
  the \emph{hom}-form identity
  \[
    FC.\mathrm{hom} \;\mathbin{;}\; (H1_{h \mathbin{;} f}).\mathrm{hom}
      \;=\; \bigl((H1_f).\mathrm{hom} \,\triangleright\, \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_h\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \bigl(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi_f \,\triangleleft\, (H1_h).\mathrm{hom}\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; (\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,\varphi_f\,\varphi_h).\mathrm{hom} ,
  \]
  stated as a faithful \(\forall\)-strengthening over the three pushforward adjunctions
  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_g \dashv \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi_g\)
  (\(g \in \{f,\, h,\, h\mathbin{;}f\}\)); the consumer instantiates it with the matching
  \(\mathtt{pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\) adjunctions. Here \(\triangleright\) is right-whiskering
  (\(\alpha \triangleright F = \mathtt{whiskerRight}\,\alpha\,F\)) and \(\triangleleft\) left-whiskering
  (\(F \triangleleft \alpha = \mathtt{whiskerLeft}\,F\,\alpha\)). Inverting the identity (swap \(f\)
  and \(h\) in the two middle legs and replace \((\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,\varphi_f\,\varphi_h).\mathrm{hom}\)
  by its \(\mathrm{inv}\)) yields the form in which \(\theta\)'s \(H1\)-prefix is recombined inside
  \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_restrict}.
  This is the dual-flank analogue of \cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_pullbackcomp_inv}
  (\(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_pullbackComp\_inv}\)) and of
  \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_restrictFunctorComp\_inv}\)
  (\cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_restrictfunctorcomp_inv}). This is the \(H1\)-flank of the cocycle
  \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_restrict}.
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison, lem:presheafofmodules_pullbackcomp, lem:scheme_modules_restrictfunctorcomp, lem:conjugate_equiv_pullbackcomp_inv, lem:adjunction_leftadjointuniq_trans, lem:conjugate_equiv_restrictfunctorcomp_inv, lem:leftadjointuniq_leftadjointcompiso_comm}
  \leanok
  Each \(H1_g\) is a \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) comparison of two left adjoints of the shared right
  adjoint \(\mathtt{pushforward}\). The composite-immersion comparison \(H1_{h \mathbin{;} f}\)
  decomposes by transitivity of \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\)
  (\cref{lem:adjunction_leftadjointuniq_trans}) once the composite pullback
  \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi_{h \mathbin{;} f}\) is identified with the iterated pullback
  \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi_h \circ \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi_f\) through
  \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,\varphi_f\,\varphi_h\) (\cref{lem:presheafofmodules_pullbackcomp}), and the
  composite pushforward \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_{h \mathbin{;} f}\) with the iterated pushforward
  through \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,h\,f\) (equivalently \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\),
  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_restrictfunctorcomp}). Because \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\) is \emph{defined} as
  the left-adjoint composition isomorphism \(\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\), the reconciliation of these
  two pseudofunctor isos on the conjugate is exactly \cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_pullbackcomp_inv},
  instantiated at the present adjunctions; substituting it collapses the conjugate to the displayed
  three-factor chain. Concretely, this is a direct instantiation of the abstract \(H1\) cocycle
  \cref{lem:leftadjointuniq_leftadjointcompiso_comm} at the three pushforward/pullback adjunction pairs,
  with \(F_\bullet = \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\), \(P_\bullet = \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) and the
  shared composition iso \(e = \mathtt{pushforwardComp}\,\varphi_f\,\varphi_h\); the \(P\)-side
  \(\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\) is definitionally \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,\varphi_f\,\varphi_h\).
nodes.lean_source:
lemma presheafDualH1Cocycle {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (h : Z ⟶ Y) (f : Y ⟶ X)
    [IsOpenImmersion h] [IsOpenImmersion f] :
    let φRf := (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom
    let αf : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf := { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv, naturality := fun _ _ i => f.appIso_inv_naturality i }
    let βf : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
      Functor.whiskerRight αf (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
    let φRh := (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom h).hom
    let αh : Z.presheaf ⟶ h.opensFunctor.op ⋙ Y.presheaf := { app := fun U => (h.appIso U.unop).inv, naturality := fun _ _ i => h.appIso_inv_naturality i }
    let βh : Z.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ h.opensFunctor.op ⋙ Y.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
      Functor.whiskerRight αh (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
    let φRhf := (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (h ≫ f)).hom
    let αhf : Z.presheaf ⟶ (h ≫ f).opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
      { app := fun U => ((h ≫ f).appIso U.unop).inv
        naturality := fun _ _ i => (h ≫ f).appIso_inv_naturality i }
    let βhf : Z.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ (h ≫ f).opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
      Functor.whiskerRight αhf (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
    ∀ (hadjf : PresheafOfModules.pushforward βf ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward φRf)
      (hadjh : PresheafOfModules.pushforward βh ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward φRh)
      (hadjhf : PresheafOfModules.pushforward βhf ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward φRhf),
    (Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso hadjf hadjh hadjhf
        (PresheafOfModules.pushforwardComp φRf φRh)).hom ≫
      (hadjhf.leftAdjointUniq (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φRhf)).hom =
    Functor.whiskerRight
        (hadjf.leftAdjointUniq (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φRf)).hom
        (PresheafOfModules.pushforward βh) ≫
      Functor.whiskerLeft (PresheafOfModules.pullback φRf)
        (hadjh.leftAdjointUniq (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φRh)).hom ≫
      (PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp φRf φRh).hom := by
  intro φRf αf βf φRh αh βh φRhf αhf βhf hadjf hadjh hadjhf
  exact Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq_leftAdjointCompIso_comm hadjf hadjh hadjhf
    (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φRf)
    (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φRh)
    (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φRhf)
    (PresheafOfModules.pushforwardComp φRf φRh)

nodes.statement:
ok
[Generic \(H1\)-cancellation skeleton for the c.2 cocycle]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Work in an arbitrary category \(C\) with objects \(A_1, \dots, A_9\) and the morphisms appearing in
  the c.2 reduction. Given the eight structural facts of that reduction --- the two iso cancellations
  \(\mathtt{aHinv} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{aH} = \mathbf{1}\) (of \(H1_{h \mathbin{;} f}\)) and
  \(\mathtt{fcinv} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{fc} = \mathbf{1}\) (of \(FC\)), the abstract \(H1\) cocycle
  \(\mathtt{fc} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{aH} = \mathtt{phf} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{hh} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{pc}\)
  (instantiating \cref{lem:presheafdual_h1_cocycle}), the \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\) cancellation
  \(\mathtt{pc} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{p0} = \mathbf{1}\), the single \(H1_h.\mathrm{hom}\) naturality
  slide \(\mathtt{hh} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{Pfhif} = \mathtt{Pushhif} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{hhdmf}\), the
  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_h\)-functoriality fold
  \(\mathtt{phf} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{Pushhif} = \mathtt{pushSDTf}\), the \(H1_h\) round-trip
  \(\mathtt{hhdmf} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{Hhinv} = \mathbf{1}\), and the residual \((**)\)
  \(\mathtt{fc} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{s} = \mathtt{pushSDTf} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sDTh} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{p3}\)
  --- the c.2 goal identity
  \(\mathtt{aHinv} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{s}
    = \mathtt{p0} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{Pfhif} \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{Hhinv} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sDTh}) \mathbin{;} \mathtt{p3}\)
  holds. This is the instance-agnostic engine that performs the entire \(H1\)-cancellation of
  \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_restrict}; the consumer supplies its eight hypotheses at
  the concrete pushforward/pullback morphisms (the cocycle hypothesis being
  \cref{lem:presheafdual_h1_cocycle}, itself \cref{lem:leftadjointuniq_leftadjointcompiso_comm} at the
  three pushforward adjunctions, reconciled by \cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointcompiso_hom}).
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{lem:presheafdual_h1_cocycle, lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointcompiso_hom, lem:leftadjointuniq_leftadjointcompiso_comm}
  \leanok
  Pure associativity and identity rewriting. Pre-compose the cocycle goal with \(\mathtt{fc}\) and use
  the cocycle hypothesis together with the residual \((**)\); the \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\)
  cancellation, the \(H1_h\) naturality slide, the \(H1_h\) round-trip and the functoriality fold then
  collapse the middle in turn, yielding \(\mathtt{fc} \mathbin{;} (\text{RHS}) = \mathtt{fc} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{s}\).
  Cancelling the iso \(\mathtt{fc}\) (via \(\mathtt{fcinv}\)) and then the iso \(\mathtt{aH}\) (via
  \(\mathtt{aHinv}\)) gives the stated identity.
nodes.lean_source:
private lemma c2_assemble {C : Type*} [Category C]
    {A1 A2 A3 A4 A5 A6 A7 A8 A9 : C}
    (aHinv : A1 ⟶ A2) (aH : A2 ⟶ A1) (s : A2 ⟶ A3)
    (fc : A4 ⟶ A2) (fcinv : A2 ⟶ A4)
    (p0 : A1 ⟶ A5) (pc : A5 ⟶ A1)
    (phf : A4 ⟶ A6) (hh : A6 ⟶ A5) (Pfhif : A5 ⟶ A7)
    (Hhinv : A7 ⟶ A8) (sDTh : A8 ⟶ A9) (p3 : A9 ⟶ A3)
    (pushSDTf : A4 ⟶ A8) (Pushhif : A6 ⟶ A8) (hhdmf : A8 ⟶ A7)
    (h_aHinv : aHinv ≫ aH = 𝟙 A1)
    (h_fcinv : fcinv ≫ fc = 𝟙 A2)
    (hcoc : fc ≫ aH = phf ≫ hh ≫ pc)
    (h_pc : pc ≫ p0 = 𝟙 A5)
    (hnat : hh ≫ Pfhif = Pushhif ≫ hhdmf)
    (hfold : phf ≫ Pushhif = pushSDTf)
    (h_hh2 : hhdmf ≫ Hhinv = 𝟙 A8)
    (hstar : fc ≫ s = pushSDTf ≫ sDTh ≫ p3) :
    aHinv ≫ s = p0 ≫ Pfhif ≫ (Hhinv ≫ sDTh) ≫ p3 := by
  have key : fc ≫ aH ≫ p0 ≫ Pfhif ≫ (Hhinv ≫ sDTh) ≫ p3 = fc ≫ s := by
    rw [hstar, ← Category.assoc fc aH, hcoc]
    simp only [Category.assoc]
    rw [← Category.assoc pc p0, h_pc, Category.id_comp,
        ← Category.assoc hh Pfhif, hnat]
    simp only [Category.assoc]
    rw [← Category.assoc hhdmf Hhinv, h_hh2, Category.id_comp,
        ← Category.assoc phf Pushhif, hfold]
  have hX : aH ≫ p0 ≫ Pfhif ≫ (Hhinv ≫ sDTh) ≫ p3 = s := by
    have h2 := congrArg (fcinv ≫ ·) key
    simp only [← Category.assoc, h_fcinv, Category.id_comp] at h2
    simpa using h2
  rw [← hX, ← Category.assoc aHinv aH, h_aHinv, Category.id_comp]

nodes.title: Bridge dual-(c): cocycle law of the presheaf dual base-change comparison \(\theta\) over \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\)
nodes.statement:
ok
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) be the chart morphism, \(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\), and
  \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The presheaf dual base-change comparison \(\theta\)
  (\cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison}) satisfies a \emph{cocycle} (composition) law over the
  immersion factorisation, in the \emph{exact dual shape} of the tensor composition coherence
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_basechange}: the \(\iota_V\)-instance \(\theta_{\iota_V, M}\) factors
  through the \(j\)-pullback of the \(\iota_U\)-instance and the \(j\)-instance \emph{at the pushforward
  object} \((\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_{\iota_U}).\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\) --- the
  base-change image of \(M_{\mathrm{val}}\), \emph{not} the restricted module \(M|_{\iota_U}\) ---
  conjugated by the presheaf pullback pseudofunctoriality iso \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,j\,\iota_U\) on the
  source and by its contravariant transport on the dual target:
  \[
    \theta_{\iota_V, M}
      = (\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,j\,\iota_U).\mathrm{inv}_{\,\mathtt{dual}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}
        \;\mathbin{;}\; (\mathtt{pullback}\,j)\bigl(\theta_{\iota_U, M}\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \theta_{j,\ M|_{\iota_U}}
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\bigl(\mathtt{forget}.\mathrm{mapIso}\,
          ((\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,j\,\iota_U).\mathrm{app}\,M)\bigr).\mathrm{hom}.
  \]
  Stating the middle factor at the restricted module \(M|_{\iota_U}\) is type-correct because the
  object-identification iso \(\mathtt{pushforwardObjValRestrictIso}\)
  (\cref{def:pushforward_obj_val_restrict_iso}, bridge c.1) is \(\mathtt{Iso.refl}\): the underlying
  presheaf \((M|_{\iota_U})_{\mathrm{val}}\) is \emph{definitionally} the pushforward object
  \((\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_{\iota_U}).\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\) (the presheaf-level pushforward
  composition being the identity), so the codomain of \((\mathtt{pullback}\,j)(\theta_{\iota_U, M})\)
  --- namely
  \((\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi_j).\mathrm{obj}\bigl(\mathtt{dual}((\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi_{\iota_U}).\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}})\bigr)\)
  --- already matches the domain of \(\theta_{j,\ M|_{\iota_U}}\) with no intervening transport. This is
  the form consumed by \cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso_restrict_compat}. On the dual (target) flank the
  contravariant transport is realised by \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\)
  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}) of the forgetful image of the restriction-composition iso
  \((\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,j\,\iota_U).\mathrm{app}\,M\)
  (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_restrictfunctorcomp}); this pseudofunctoriality leg appears in place of the
  \(\varrho \otimes \varrho\) of the tensor side. This is the dual analogue of the composition role of
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural} inside \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_basechange}.
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison, lem:presheafdual_h1_cocycle, lem:slice_dual_transport_app_apply, def:pushforward_obj_val_restrict_iso, lem:presheafofmodules_pullbackcomp, lem:scheme_modules_restrictfunctorcomp, def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso, lem:image_preimage_of_le, lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural, lem:leftadjointcompiso_hom_app, lem:slice_dual_transport_comp, lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointcompiso_hom, lem:c2_assemble, lem:pushforwardpushforwardadj_unit_app_app_apply, lem:pushforwardpushforwardadj_counit_app_app_apply, lem:dual_unit_ring_swap_apply, lem:scheme_modules_comp_appiso}
  \leanok
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  The cocycle \emph{factors cleanly}: every \(H1\) factor cancels against the abstract \(H1\) cocycle
  (a) (\cref{lem:presheafdual_h1_cocycle}) together with a single ordinary naturality square, leaving
  one isolated pushforward-flank identity that touches \(\mathtt{sliceDualTransport}\)'s internals.
  Working sectionwise (each \(\theta.\mathrm{hom}\) is a presheaf morphism), abbreviate
  \(dM = \mathtt{dual}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\),
  \(\mathtt{sDT}_g = \mathtt{isoMk}(\mathtt{sliceDualTransport}\,g).\mathrm{hom}\),
  \(FC = \mathtt{pushforwardComp}\), and
  \(\varrho = (\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,j\,\iota_U).\mathrm{app}\,M\), and expand each instance via
  \cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison} as
  \(\theta_g = (H1_g)^{-1} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sDT}_g\). The reduction runs in six steps.
  \begin{enumerate}
    \item \textbf{Strip the leading inverse.} Left-multiply both sides by
      \(H1_{\iota_V}.\mathrm{hom}\) (using the isomorphism inverse-composition identity); the left side
      becomes \(\mathtt{sDT}_{\iota_V}\) and the right side acquires the prefix
      \(H1_{\iota_V}.\mathrm{hom}\).
    \item \textbf{Expose the cocycle prefix.} Left-multiply by the \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\) counit
      \(FC.\mathrm{hom}_{dM}\), legitimate because it is an isomorphism (hence an epimorphism); the
      left flank now reads \(FC.\mathrm{hom}_{dM} \mathbin{;} H1_{\iota_V}.\mathrm{hom}_{dM}\).
    \item \textbf{Substitute cocycle (a).} By the abstract \(H1\) cocycle
      \cref{lem:presheafdual_h1_cocycle} this prefix equals
      \((\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_j).\mathrm{map}\,(H1_{\iota_U}.\mathrm{hom})
        \mathbin{;} H1_j.\mathrm{hom}_{(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_{\iota_U})\,dM}
        \mathbin{;} \mathtt{pullbackComp}.\mathrm{hom}_{dM}\),
      regrouping the \(H1_{\iota_U}\) factor across the pushforward middle functor on the source flank
      (\(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\), \cref{lem:presheafofmodules_pullbackcomp}).
    \item \textbf{Cancel \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\).} The trailing
      \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}.\mathrm{hom}_{dM}\) meets the leading
      \((\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,j\,\iota_U).\mathrm{inv}_{dM}\) of the right side and the two cancel
      (as the hom-inv identity of an isomorphism).
    \item \textbf{Slide \(H1_j\) by one naturality.} The factor \(H1_j.\mathrm{hom}\) is moved past
      \((\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi_j).\mathrm{map}\,(\theta_{\iota_U}.\mathrm{hom})\) by a \emph{single}
      ordinary naturality square of the natural transformation \(H1_j.\mathrm{hom}\) at
      \(\theta_{\iota_U}.\mathrm{hom}\) --- this is the only ``slide'', and it is plain
      naturality of \(H1_j.\mathrm{hom}\) as a natural transformation, not a bespoke multi-step calculation.
    \item \textbf{Collapse the \(H1\) round-trips.} The hom-inv identities of
      \(H1_{\iota_U}\) and \(H1_j\) annihilate the remaining \(H1\) factors, leaving the sole residual
      \begin{equation}
        FC.\mathrm{hom}_{dM} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sDT}_{\iota_V}
          \;=\;
        (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_j).\mathrm{map}(\mathtt{sDT}_{\iota_U})
          \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sDT}_j(M|_{\iota_U})
          \mathbin{;} \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}(\mathtt{forget}.\mathrm{mapIso}\,\varrho).\mathrm{hom}.
        \tag{$**$}
      \end{equation}
  \end{enumerate}
  The residual \((**)\) is the pushforward-flank pseudofunctoriality of
  \(\mathtt{sliceDualTransport}\) --- the only step that touches the transport's internals --- and it
  plays the dual role of \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural} inside the proven tensor analogue
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_basechange}.

  \textbf{The leading factor \(FC.\mathrm{hom}\) is explicit, not opaque.} Here
  \(FC = \mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\) of the three pushforward adjunctions. It is \emph{correct} that
  \(FC.\mathrm{hom}\) is \emph{not} definitionally equal to the concrete presheaf pushforward-composition
  iso \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\): the domain
  \((\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_j \circ \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_{\iota_U}).\mathrm{obj}\,dM\) and the
  codomain \((\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_{\iota_V}).\mathrm{obj}\,dM\) are themselves not defeq, because
  the \(\mathtt{opensFunctor}\) of a composite open immersion is not the composite of the
  \(\mathtt{opensFunctor}\)s on the nose. This is \emph{not}, however, an obstruction: \(FC.\mathrm{hom}\)
  is \emph{fully explicit} via the Mathlib component formula
  \(\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso\_hom\_app}\) (\cref{lem:leftadjointcompiso_hom_app}), which expands
  \(FC.\mathrm{hom}.\mathrm{app}\,X\) as the unit of the composite adjunction pushed through the two left
  adjoints, the reconciling \(e.\mathrm{inv}\), and the two counits. With
  \(e = \mathtt{pushforwardComp} = \mathtt{Iso.refl}\) the middle \(e.\mathrm{inv}\) factor drops, leaving
  an explicit unit/counit composite; the residual \((**)\) is reachable via this formula.

  \textbf{The close route.} Expose \(FC.\mathrm{hom}\) through the component formula
  \cref{lem:leftadjointcompiso_hom_app}. (The mate characterisation
  \cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointcompiso_hom} is an alternative reading --- it identifies the
  conjugate \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv}\,FC.\mathrm{hom} = e.\mathrm{inv} = \mathbf 1\), but delivers only
  the mate, not the section-level component directly.) After
  substituting the explicit unit/counit form of \(FC.\mathrm{hom}\) and cancelling all
  \(\mathtt{appIso}\)/\(\mathtt{dualUnitRingSwap}\) change-of-rings layers --- the structure-ring layers
  reconciling through the cocycle \(\mathtt{comp\_appIso}\) (\(\mathtt{Scheme.Hom.comp\_appIso}\),
  \((h \mathbin{;} f).\mathtt{appIso}\,U = X.\mathrm{presheaf}.\mathrm{mapIso}(\mathtt{eqToHom})^{\mathrm{op}}
  \mathbin{;} f.\mathtt{appIso}(h.\mathtt{opensFunctor}\,U) \mathbin{;} h.\mathtt{appIso}\,U\)) ---
  the residual \((**)\) reduces to a single \emph{thin-poset / \(\mathtt{opensFunctor}\)-composition
  coherence}. The leading \(FC.\mathrm{hom}\) is handled sectionwise via \cref{lem:leftadjointcompiso_hom_app}: it is the composite of the pushforward adjunction's
  unit and two counits, and \emph{each} of these, evaluated at a section, is --- by the triangle-component
  identities \cref{lem:pushforwardpushforwardadj_unit_app_app_apply} and
  \cref{lem:pushforwardpushforwardadj_counit_app_app_apply} --- precisely a presheaf restriction map
  \(X.\mathrm{presheaf}.\mathrm{map}(-)^{\mathrm{op}}\) of an \(\mathrm{Opens}\,X\) morphism. This form is
  reached \emph{definitionally} (the units/counits are restriction maps on the nose); direct unfolding
  of \(\mathtt{pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\) would reintroduce the
  \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) carrier diamond. Folding the three restriction maps by functoriality
  yields one \(X.\mathrm{presheaf}.\mathrm{map}(\text{composite})^{\mathrm{op}}\); the two resulting
  slice objects share domain (\((j\mathbin{;}\iota_U).\mathtt{opensFunctor} =
  \iota_U.\mathtt{opensFunctor}\circ j.\mathtt{opensFunctor}\), \(\mathtt{comp\_image}\)) and codomain,
  hence are \(\mathtt{Subsingleton}\)-equal in the thin poset \(\mathrm{Opens}\,X\), and the residual
  closes. The slice-side split \cref{lem:slice_dual_transport_comp} supplies the matching
  \(\mathtt{sDT}_{j\mathbin{;}\iota_U}\) factorisation on that flank. \emph{The mate \(FC\) must remain
  the \(\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\)}: it cannot be replaced by the concrete
  \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\,\beta_j\,\beta_{\iota_U}\) --- their codomain functors differ by the
  \(\mathtt{comp\_appIso}\) reindex (so the two are not even type-compatible), and the swap would break
  the proven cocycle~(a) \cref{lem:presheafdual_h1_cocycle}, which needs both flanks to be
  \(\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\) of the \emph{same} \(e\).
  The sectionwise content, supplied by \cref{lem:slice_dual_transport_comp}, runs
  through the forward apply lemma \cref{lem:slice_dual_transport_app_apply}: on each open \(W\) of \(V\)
  and each dual section it rewrites the composite \(\mathtt{sDT}\) factor into its explicit
  \(\mathtt{dualUnitRingSwap}\,(j\mathbin{;}\iota_U)\)-conjugated reindex; by the action
  \cref{lem:dual_unit_ring_swap_apply} that swap is the \(\mathrm{hom}\)-direction of the composite
  structure-ring iso, and the cocycle \(\mathtt{comp\_appIso}\) (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_comp_appiso})
  splits it into the two single-immersion swaps of \(\iota_U\) and \(j\), reindexed by the direct-image
  composition \((j\mathbin{;}\iota_U).\mathtt{opensFunctor} =
  \iota_U.\mathtt{opensFunctor}\circ j.\mathtt{opensFunctor}\) (\(\mathtt{comp\_image}\)). The
  module-argument naturality of \(\theta\)
  (\cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural}) together with poset-thinness of
  \(\mathrm{Opens}\) --- the consumer reads everything over the single open \(j\mathbin{;}\iota_U=\iota_V\)
  (\cref{lem:image_preimage_of_le}) --- makes the assembly canonical, and the target-flank reindexing is
  realised by \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\) (\cref{def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}) of the forgetful image
  of \(\varrho\) (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_restrictfunctorcomp}). The middle factor already sits at
  \(M|_{\iota_U}\) because the object identification \(\mathtt{pushforwardObjValRestrictIso}\)
  (\cref{def:pushforward_obj_val_restrict_iso}, c.1) is \(\mathtt{Iso.refl}\): the underlying presheaf
  of \(M|_{\iota_U}\) is definitionally the pushforward object, so no intervening transport appears.

  The entire six-step \(H1\)-cancellation above is carried out by a single generic,
  instance-agnostic skeleton lemma \(\mathtt{c2\_assemble}\) (\cref{lem:c2_assemble}), stated over an
  arbitrary ambient category. It takes the structural facts of the reduction as hypotheses --- the two
  iso cancellations of \(H1_{h \mathbin{;} f}\) and \(FC\) at \(dM\), the abstract \(H1\) cocycle~(a)
  (\cref{lem:presheafdual_h1_cocycle}), the \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\) cancellation, the single
  \(H1_h.\mathrm{hom}\) naturality, the \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_h\)-functoriality fold, the
  \(H1_h\) round-trip at \(d(M|_{\iota_U})\), and the residual \((**)\) --- and assembles the c.2
  identity from them by associativity and identity rewriting alone. The abstract identity transfers
  across the definitional boundary between \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) and
  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\) because the skeleton is stated for abstract morphisms in an arbitrary
  category: instantiating at the concrete pullback/pushforward morphisms specialises the identity
  directly to the c.2 goal, and the definitional equality between the two categories is absorbed into
  the abstract hypothesis statements rather than appearing as an additional verification condition.
nodes.lean_source:
lemma presheafDualPullbackComparison_restrict {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (h : Z ⟶ Y) (f : Y ⟶ X)
    [IsOpenImmersion h] [IsOpenImmersion f] (M : X.Modules) :
    presheafDualPullbackComparison (h ≫ f) M =
      (PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom
          (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom h).hom).symm.app
            (PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := X.presheaf) M.val)
      ≪≫ (PresheafOfModules.pullback (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom h).hom).mapIso
            (presheafDualPullbackComparison f M)
      ≪≫ presheafDualPullbackComparison h (M.restrict f)
      ≪≫ PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso
            ((SheafOfModules.forget Z.ringCatSheaf).mapIso
              ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorComp h f).app M)) := by

  apply Iso.ext

  simp only [presheafDualPullbackComparison, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.symm_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom]

  let hadjf : PresheafOfModules.pushforward
        (Functor.whiskerRight ({ app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv, naturality := fun _ _ i => f.appIso_inv_naturality i } :
          Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf) (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) ⊣
      PresheafOfModules.pushforward (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom :=
    PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction _ _
      (by ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x))
      (by ext U x; exact congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x))
  let hadjh : PresheafOfModules.pushforward
        (Functor.whiskerRight ({ app := fun U => (h.appIso U.unop).inv, naturality := fun _ _ i => h.appIso_inv_naturality i } :
          Z.presheaf ⟶ h.opensFunctor.op ⋙ Y.presheaf) (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) ⊣
      PresheafOfModules.pushforward (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom h).hom :=
    PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj h.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction _ _
      (by ext U x; exact congr($((h.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x))
      (by ext U x; exact congr($(h.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x))
  let αhf : Z.presheaf ⟶ (h ≫ f).opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
    { app := fun U => ((h ≫ f).appIso U.unop).inv
      naturality := fun _ _ i => (h ≫ f).appIso_inv_naturality i }
  let hadjhf : PresheafOfModules.pushforward
        (Functor.whiskerRight αhf (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) ⊣
      PresheafOfModules.pushforward (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (h ≫ f)).hom :=
    PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj (h ≫ f).isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction _ _
      (by ext U x; exact congr($(((h ≫ f).app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x))
      (by ext U x; exact congr($((h ≫ f).appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x))
  have hcoc := presheafDualH1Cocycle h f hadjf hadjh hadjhf

  let βh : Z.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ (Hom.opensFunctor h).op ⋙ Y.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
    Functor.whiskerRight ({ app := fun U => (h.appIso U.unop).inv, naturality := fun _ _ i => h.appIso_inv_naturality i } :
      Z.presheaf ⟶ (Hom.opensFunctor h).op ⋙ Y.presheaf) (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
  let βf : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ (Hom.opensFunctor f).op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
    Functor.whiskerRight ({ app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv, naturality := fun _ _ i => f.appIso_inv_naturality i } :
      Y.presheaf ⟶ (Hom.opensFunctor f).op ⋙ X.presheaf) (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
  let H1hf := hadjhf.leftAdjointUniq
    (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (h ≫ f)).hom)
  let H1f := hadjf.leftAdjointUniq
    (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom)
  let H1h := hadjh.leftAdjointUniq
    (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom h).hom)
  let FC := hadjf.leftAdjointCompIso hadjh hadjhf
    (PresheafOfModules.pushforwardComp (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom
      (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom h).hom)
  let pbC := PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom
    (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom h).hom
  let iMf := PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun V => sliceDualTransport f M V)
    (by intro V W g; subsingleton)
  let gf := (H1f.app M.val.dual).inv ≫ iMf.hom
  refine c2_assemble
    (aHinv := (H1hf.app M.val.dual).inv) (aH := (H1hf.app M.val.dual).hom)
    (s := (PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun V => sliceDualTransport (h ≫ f) M V)
      (by intro V W g; subsingleton)).hom)
    (fc := FC.hom.app M.val.dual) (fcinv := FC.inv.app M.val.dual)
    (p0 := (pbC.symm.app M.val.dual).hom) (pc := pbC.hom.app M.val.dual)
    (phf := (PresheafOfModules.pushforward βh).map (H1f.hom.app M.val.dual))
    (hh := H1h.hom.app
      ((PresheafOfModules.pullback (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom).obj M.val.dual))
    (Pfhif := (PresheafOfModules.pullback (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom h).hom).map gf)
    (Hhinv := (H1h.app (M.restrict f).val.dual).inv)
    (sDTh := (PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun V => sliceDualTransport h (M.restrict f) V)
      (by intro V W g; subsingleton)).hom)
    (p3 := (PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso
      ((SheafOfModules.forget Z.ringCatSheaf).mapIso ((restrictFunctorComp h f).app M))).hom)
    (pushSDTf := (PresheafOfModules.pushforward βh).map iMf.hom)
    (Pushhif := (PresheafOfModules.pushforward βh).map gf)
    (hhdmf := H1h.hom.app (M.restrict f).val.dual)
    (h_aHinv := (H1hf.app M.val.dual).inv_hom_id)
    (h_fcinv := FC.inv_hom_id_app M.val.dual)
    (hcoc := NatTrans.congr_app hcoc M.val.dual)
    (h_pc := pbC.hom_inv_id_app M.val.dual)
    (hnat := (H1h.hom.naturality gf).symm)
    (hfold := ?hfold)
    (h_hh2 := H1h.hom_inv_id_app (M.restrict f).val.dual)
    (hstar := ?hstar)
  case hfold =>
    rw [← Functor.map_comp]
    refine congrArg (PresheafOfModules.pushforward βh).map ?_
    change H1f.hom.app M.val.dual ≫ gf = iMf.hom
    rw [← Category.assoc, show H1f.hom.app M.val.dual ≫ (H1f.app M.val.dual).inv = 𝟙 _ from
      (H1f.app M.val.dual).hom_inv_id, Category.id_comp]
  case hstar =>

    apply PresheafOfModules.hom_ext
    intro V
    apply ModuleCat.hom_ext
    apply LinearMap.ext
    intro φ
    apply PresheafOfModules.hom_ext
    intro W
    apply ModuleCat.hom_ext
    apply LinearMap.ext
    intro z
    simp only [PresheafOfModules.comp_app, PresheafOfModules.isoMk_hom_app,
      ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply]
    erw [sliceDualTransport_app_apply (h ≫ f) M V ((FC.hom.app M.val.dual).app V φ) W z]
    rw [dualUnitRingSwap_apply]

    erw [PresheafOfModules.pushforward_map_app_apply βh iMf.hom V φ]
    simp only [iMf, PresheafOfModules.isoMk_hom_app]

    rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_appIso h f (unop W).left]
    simp only [Iso.trans_hom, CommRingCat.hom_comp, RingHom.comp_apply, Functor.mapIso_hom]

    erw [sliceDualTransport_app_apply h (M.restrict f) V _ W]
    rw [dualUnitRingSwap_apply]
    erw [sliceDualTransport_app_apply f M (op ((Hom.opensFunctor h).obj (unop V))) φ
      (op (Over.mk ((Hom.opensFunctor h).map (unop W).hom)))]
    rw [dualUnitRingSwap_apply]

    refine congrArg (Hom.appIso h (unop W).left).hom.hom ?_
    refine congrArg (Hom.appIso f ((Hom.opensFunctor h).obj (unop W).left)).hom.hom ?_

    simp only [FC]
    rw [Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso_hom_app]

    simp only [PresheafOfModules.pushforwardComp, Iso.refl_inv, NatTrans.id_app,
      CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, Category.id_comp, Category.comp_id,
      PresheafOfModules.comp_app, ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply]

    erw [PresheafOfModules.pushforward_map_app_apply βh
          ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward βf).map (hadjhf.unit.app M.val.dual)) V φ,
        PresheafOfModules.pushforward_map_app_apply βf (hadjhf.unit.app M.val.dual)
          (op ((Hom.opensFunctor h).obj (unop V))) φ]

    erw [PresheafOfModules.pushforward_map_app_apply βh
          (hadjf.counit.app ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom h).hom).obj
            ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward (Functor.whiskerRight αhf (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).obj
              M.val.dual))) V _]

    simp only [hadjf, hadjh, hadjhf]

    erw [PresheafOfModules.ppadj_unit_app_app_apply
          (adj := (h ≫ f).isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction)
          (φ := Functor.whiskerRight αhf (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
          (ψ := (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (h ≫ f)).hom) (M := M.val.dual)
          (U := op ((Hom.opensFunctor f).obj ((Hom.opensFunctor h).obj (unop V))))]
    erw [PresheafOfModules.ppadj_counit_app_app_apply
          (adj := f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction) (φ := βf)
          (ψ := (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom)
          (N := (PresheafOfModules.pushforward (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom h).hom).obj
            ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward (Functor.whiskerRight αhf (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).obj
              M.val.dual))
          (U := op ((Hom.opensFunctor h).obj (unop V)))]
    erw [PresheafOfModules.ppadj_counit_app_app_apply
          (adj := h.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction) (φ := βh)
          (ψ := (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom h).hom)
          (N := (PresheafOfModules.pushforward (Functor.whiskerRight αhf (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).obj
            M.val.dual)
          (U := V)]

    erw [PresheafOfModules.pushforward_obj_map_apply
          (φ := Functor.whiskerRight αhf (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (M := M.val.dual)
          (f := (h.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction.unit.app (unop V)).op)]
    erw [PresheafOfModules.pushforward_obj_map_apply (φ := (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom h).hom)
          (M := (PresheafOfModules.pushforward (Functor.whiskerRight αhf (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).obj
            M.val.dual)
          (f := (f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction.unit.app
            (unop (op ((Hom.opensFunctor h).obj (unop V))))).op)]
    erw [PresheafOfModules.pushforward_obj_map_apply
          (φ := Functor.whiskerRight αhf (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (M := M.val.dual)
          (f := ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map h.base).map
            (f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction.unit.app
              (unop (op ((Hom.opensFunctor h).obj (unop V))))).op.unop).op)]

    erw [PresheafOfModules.dual_map_app_apply, PresheafOfModules.dual_map_app_apply,
      PresheafOfModules.dual_map_app_apply]

    simp only [Over.map_obj_left, Over.map_obj_hom, Functor.op_obj, Functor.const_obj_obj,
      Over.mk_left, Over.mk_hom, unop_op, Over.forget_obj]

    have hle : ((Hom.opensFunctor f).obj ((Hom.opensFunctor h).obj (unop W).left))
        ≤ ((Hom.opensFunctor (h ≫ f)).obj (unop W).left) :=
      le_of_eq (Scheme.Hom.comp_image h f (unop W).left).symm

    have key := hstar_naturality M φ
      (Over.homMk (homOfLE hle) (Subsingleton.elim _ _) :
        (Over.mk ((Hom.opensFunctor f).map ((Hom.opensFunctor h).map (unop W).hom)) :
            Over (f ''ᵁ h ''ᵁ unop V)) ⟶
          (Over.map ((h ≫ f).isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction.counit.app
                (f ''ᵁ h ''ᵁ unop V))).obj
            ((Over.map ((Hom.opensFunctor (h ≫ f)).map ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map h.base).map
                  (f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction.unit.app (h ''ᵁ unop V))))).obj
              ((Over.map ((Hom.opensFunctor (h ≫ f)).map
                    (h.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction.unit.app (unop V)))).obj
                (Over.mk ((Hom.opensFunctor (h ≫ f)).map (unop W).hom))))).op z

    convert key using 2 <;> rfl

nodes.statement:
ok
[L1: folded composition cocycle for \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\) along a composite]
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference (dual mirror of the
  tensor-flank composition law \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso_restrict_compat}).}
  Let \(f : Z \hookrightarrow Y\) and \(g : Y \hookrightarrow X\) be composable open immersions
  (\emph{general} --- not tied to the chart \(j\)) and \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). Write
  \(D(h) := \mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,h : (\mathtt{dual}\,A)|_h \cong \mathtt{dual}(A|_h)\)
  (\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso}). The dual-restriction comparison along the composite \(f \mathbin{;} g\)
  factors --- \emph{entirely at the folded Scheme-\(\mathtt{dual}\) level}, so that every junction is a
  Scheme-dual \(\leftrightarrow\) Scheme-dual identification and no \(\mathtt{sheafification}\)-colimit
  seam appears in the statement --- as
  \[
    D(f \mathbin{;} g)
      \;=\; \bigl(\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,f\,g\bigr).\mathrm{app}\,(\mathtt{dual}\,M)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; (\mathtt{restrict}\,f)\,D(g)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; D(f)\,\bigl(M|_g\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,\bigl((\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,f\,g).\mathrm{app}\,M\bigr),
  \]
  where \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,f\,g\) (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_restrictfunctorcomp}) is the
  restriction-composition pseudofunctoriality iso \(A|_{f\mathbin{;}g} \cong (A|_g)|_f\) and
  \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\) (\cref{def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}) is its contravariant dual image.
  Choosing the \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\)-induced head (rather than a \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\)
  one) keeps both flanks in the restriction world, hence syntactically folded.
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{lem:dual_restrict_iso, lem:dual_restrict_iso_eq_comparison, lem:restrictfunctorisopullback_comp_compat, lem:sheafificationcomppullback_restrict, lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_restrict, lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural, def:pushforward_obj_val_restrict_iso, def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso, lem:scheme_modules_restrictfunctorcomp}
  This is the dual mirror of the tensor-flank composition law
  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso_restrict_compat}), routed through Cone~B (internal-hom base-change);
  no ``closed functor preserves duals'' principle applies, since pullback along an open immersion is
  neither faithful nor an equivalence. The seam between the Scheme-\(\mathtt{dual}\) world and the
  sheafified presheaf-dual world is crossed \emph{inside} this proof exactly as bridges (b), (c) and the
  S2 route do --- never across the stated junction.

  By the dual analogue of Bridge B1 (\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso_eq_comparison}) each
  \(D(\cdot)\) factors as \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback} \mathbin{;}
  \mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sheafification}(\theta)\). Substituting this for
  the three instances \(D(f\mathbin{;}g)\), \(D(g)\) and \(D(f)\), the leading
  \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\) factors cancel against the
  \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\)-reindex by Bridge B2 (\cref{lem:restrictfunctorisopullback_comp_compat}),
  reducing the identity to its \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\)- and \(\theta\)-legs. The
  \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\)-cocycle over the composite is closed by bridge~(b)
  (\cref{lem:sheafificationcomppullback_restrict}); the \(\theta\)-cocycle by bridge~(c)
  (\cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_restrict}, itself through the immersion-naturality of
  \(\theta\), \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural}). Bridge~(c) delivers its middle
  \(\theta\)-factor at the pushforward object \((\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_g).\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\);
  the object-identification iso \(\mathtt{pushforwardObjValRestrictIso}\)
  (\cref{def:pushforward_obj_val_restrict_iso}, bridge c.1), which is \(\mathtt{Iso.refl}\), converts that
  factor to the \(D(f)\,(M|_g)\)-form appearing here, so the substitution is type-correct. The
  contravariant reindexing on the dual flank is the dual image
  \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\) (\cref{def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}) of
  \((\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,f\,g).\mathrm{app}\,M\)
  (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_restrictfunctorcomp}). Assembling the leg-for-leg identities yields the
  displayed composite.
nodes.lean_source:
lemma dual_restrict_iso_comp {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (f : Z ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ X)
    [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g] (M : X.Modules) :
    dual_restrict_iso (f ≫ g) M
      = (restrictFunctorComp f g).app (dual M)
          ≪≫ (restrictFunctor f).mapIso (dual_restrict_iso g M)
          ≪≫ dual_restrict_iso f (M.restrict g)
          ≪≫ dualIsoOfIso ((restrictFunctorComp f g).app M) := by

  rw [dual_restrict_iso_eq_comparison (f ≫ g) M,
    restrictFunctorIsoPullback_comp_general f g M.dual]
  simp only [Iso.trans_assoc]

  congr 1

  erw [mapIso_naturality_assoc (restrictFunctorIsoPullback f)
        ((restrictFunctorIsoPullback g).app M.dual)]

  have hR : (restrictFunctor f).mapIso (dual_restrict_iso g M)
        ≪≫ dual_restrict_iso f (M.restrict g)
      = (restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app ((dual M).restrict g)
          ≪≫ (pullback f).mapIso (dual_restrict_iso g M)
          ≪≫ ((restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app ((M.restrict g).dual)).symm
          ≪≫ dual_restrict_iso f (M.restrict g) := by
    apply Iso.ext
    simp only [Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom, Iso.symm_hom, Iso.app_hom, Iso.app_inv,
      Category.assoc]
    rw [← (restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).hom.naturality_assoc (dual_restrict_iso g M).hom]
    simp only [Iso.hom_inv_id_app_assoc]
  erw [← Iso.trans_assoc ((restrictFunctor f).mapIso (dual_restrict_iso g M))
        (dual_restrict_iso f (M.restrict g)), hR]
  simp only [Iso.trans_assoc]

  congr 1

  have hsplit : (pullback f).mapIso (dual_restrict_iso g M)
      = (pullback f).mapIso ((restrictFunctorIsoPullback g).app M.dual)
        ≪≫ (pullback f).mapIso
            ((SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom g)).app M.val.dual
            ≪≫ (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapIso
                  (presheafDualPullbackComparison g M)) :=
    Functor.mapIso_trans (pullback f) _ _
  rw [hsplit]

  refine iso_cancel_left _ _ _ _ ?_

  rw [iso_symm_fold_cancel _ _ _ (dual_restrict_iso_eq_comparison f (M.restrict g))
        (dualIsoOfIso ((restrictFunctorComp f g).app M))]

  rw [presheafDualPullbackComparison_restrict f g M]

  apply Iso.ext
  simp only [Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom, Functor.map_comp, Iso.symm_hom, Iso.app_hom]

  have ha2 :
      (SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (f ≫ g))).hom.app M.val.dual
        = (SheafOfModules.pullbackComp (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom g) (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f)).inv.app
              ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).obj M.val.dual)
          ≫ (SheafOfModules.pullback (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f)).map
              ((SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom g)).hom.app M.val.dual)
          ≫ (SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f)).hom.app
              ((PresheafOfModules.pullback (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom g).hom).obj M.val.dual)
          ≫ (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Z.ringCatSheaf.obj)).map
              ((PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom g).hom
                (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom).hom.app M.val.dual) :=
    sheafificationCompPullback_comp f g M.val.dual

  have haZc :
      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Z.ringCatSheaf.obj)).map
          ((PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom g).hom
                (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom).inv.app M.val.dual
            ≫ (PresheafOfModules.pullback (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom).map
                (presheafDualPullbackComparison g M).hom
            ≫ (presheafDualPullbackComparison f (M.restrict g)).hom
            ≫ (PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso ((SheafOfModules.forget Z.ringCatSheaf).mapIso
                ((restrictFunctorComp f g).app M))).hom)
        = (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Z.ringCatSheaf.obj)).map
            ((PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom g).hom
              (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom).inv.app M.val.dual)
          ≫ (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Z.ringCatSheaf.obj)).map
              ((PresheafOfModules.pullback (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom).map
                (presheafDualPullbackComparison g M).hom)
          ≫ (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Z.ringCatSheaf.obj)).map
              (presheafDualPullbackComparison f (M.restrict g)).hom
          ≫ (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Z.ringCatSheaf.obj)).map
              (PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso ((SheafOfModules.forget Z.ringCatSheaf).mapIso
                ((restrictFunctorComp f g).app M))).hom := by
    rw [← Functor.map_comp, ← Functor.map_comp, ← Functor.map_comp]

  have hcancel1 :
      (pullbackComp f g).hom.app M.dual
        ≫ (SheafOfModules.pullbackComp (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom g) (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f)).inv.app
            ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).obj M.val.dual) = 𝟙 _ :=
    Iso.hom_inv_id_app _ _

  have hcancelrd :
      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Z.ringCatSheaf.obj)).map
          ((PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom g).hom
            (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom).hom.app M.val.dual)
        ≫ (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Z.ringCatSheaf.obj)).map
            ((PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom g).hom
              (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom).inv.app M.val.dual) = 𝟙 _ :=
    (CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp
        (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Z.ringCatSheaf.obj)) _ _).symm.trans
      ((congrArg (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Z.ringCatSheaf.obj)).map
          (Iso.hom_inv_id_app (PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom g).hom
            (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom) M.val.dual)).trans
        (CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id
          (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Z.ringCatSheaf.obj)) _))

  have hnat :
      (SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f)).hom.app
          ((PresheafOfModules.pullback (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom g).hom).obj M.val.dual)
        ≫ (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Z.ringCatSheaf.obj)).map
            ((PresheafOfModules.pullback (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom).map
              (presheafDualPullbackComparison g M).hom)
        = (SheafOfModules.pullback (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f)).map
            ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)).map
              (presheafDualPullbackComparison g M).hom)
          ≫ (SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f)).hom.app
              (M.restrict g).val.dual :=
    ((SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f)).hom.naturality
      (presheafDualPullbackComparison g M).hom).symm
  exact dual_scp_assemble ha2 haZc hcancel1 hcancelrd hnat

set_option maxHeartbeats 6400000 in

nodes.statement:
ok
[L2: folded \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorCongr}\) coherence for \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\)]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(f, g : Y \hookrightarrow X\) be open immersions with \(f = g\) (witnessed by \(hf\)), and
  \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The dual-restriction comparison is coherent with the
  restriction congruence \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorCongr}\,hf : A|_f \cong A|_g\), at the folded
  Scheme-\(\mathtt{dual}\) level (both junctions Scheme-dual \(\leftrightarrow\) Scheme-dual, seam-free):
  \[
    \bigl(\mathtt{restrictFunctorCongr}\,hf\bigr).\mathrm{app}\,(\mathtt{dual}\,M)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,g\,M
      \;=\; \mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,f\,M
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,\bigl((\mathtt{restrictFunctorCongr}\,hf).\mathrm{app}\,M\bigr)^{-1}.
  \]
  This is the dual mirror of \(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap\_pullbackCongr}\), used to convert the
  \(\iota_V\)-leg of S3 to the composite \(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U\). \emph{(This brick is already verified
  in Lean: \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso\_pullbackCongr}\), private, axiom-clean.)}
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{lem:dual_restrict_iso, def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso, lem:dualisoofiso_refl}
  Substitute \(g := f\) along \(hf\). Then the restriction congruence collapses,
  \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorCongr}\,(\mathrm{rfl}) = \mathrm{Iso.refl}\) (section-level, since its hom is
  \(\mathrm{rfl}\)-valued on each section), so the left factor on the source flank is the identity; and on
  the dual flank \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,(\mathrm{Iso.refl}) = \mathrm{Iso.refl}\)
  (\cref{lem:dualisoofiso_refl}, \cref{def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}). Both sides reduce to
  \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,f\,M\), and the equation holds.
nodes.lean_source:
private lemma dual_restrict_iso_pullbackCongr {X Y : Scheme.{u}} {f g : Y ⟶ X}
    (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g] (M : X.Modules) :
    (restrictFunctorCongr hf).app M.dual ≪≫ dual_restrict_iso g M
      = dual_restrict_iso f M ≪≫ dualIsoOfIso ((restrictFunctorCongr hf).app M).symm := by
  subst hf

  have hc : restrictFunctorCongr (rfl : f = f) = Iso.refl (restrictFunctor f) := by
    ext M U <;> simp <;> rfl
  have hcD : (restrictFunctorCongr (rfl : f = f)).app M.dual = Iso.refl _ := by rw [hc]; rfl
  have hcM : (restrictFunctorCongr (rfl : f = f)).app M = Iso.refl _ := by rw [hc]; rfl
  rw [hcD, hcM, Iso.refl_symm, dualIsoOfIso_refl, Iso.refl_trans, Iso.trans_refl]

nodes.statement:
ok
[S3: dual-restriction comparison commutes with further restriction (clean core)]
  
  
  
  Let \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) and let \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) be the chart
  morphism above. Write \(D(f) := \mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,f :
  (\mathtt{dual}\,M)|_f \cong \mathtt{dual}\bigl(M|_f\bigr)\) (\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso}). The
  \emph{bare} dual-restriction comparison is natural under further restriction along \(j\): modulo
  the reindexing \(\varrho = \mathtt{restrictCompReindex}\,j\) (\cref{def:restrictcompreindex}),
  with its contravariant image \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,\varrho\)
  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}) on the dual flank,
  \[
    D(\iota_V)
      \;=\; \varrho_{\mathtt{dual}\,M}
        \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,D(\iota_U)
        \mathbin{;} \mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,j\,\bigl(M|_{\iota_U}\bigr)
        \mathbin{;} \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,\varrho_M .
  \]
  This square proves \emph{only} the \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\) immersion-naturality; the
  trivialisation step's \((\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,e^M)^{-1}\) transport and the datum refinement
  \(e^M \mapsto \mathtt{restrictIsoUnitOfLE}\,h_{VU}\,e^M\) are \emph{not} part of the claim --- they
  telescope in the parent (\cref{lem:trivialisation_restrict_compat}).
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{lem:dual_restrict_iso, lem:dual_restrict_iso_comp, lem:dual_restrict_iso_pullbackcongr, lem:dualisoofiso_trans, def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso, def:restrictcompreindex, lem:image_preimage_of_le}
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  The square is now a short assembly of the folded composition cocycle L1
  (\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso_comp}) and the folded congruence coherence L2
  (\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso_pullbackcongr}); the seam-crossing work (dual-B1, B2, bridges (b), (c),
  c.1) has been moved into L1's proof, where the unfolding to
  \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}
  \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sheafification}(\theta)\) is confined. The proof is the dual-flank mirror of S2,
  routed through Cone~B (internal-hom base-change), not through any sheaf-level monoidal structure.

  Specialise L1 (\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso_comp}) at \((f, g) = (j, \iota_U)\). The equality of opens
  \(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\) (\(hjι\), \cref{lem:image_preimage_of_le}) converts the
  \(\iota_V\)-leg \(D(\iota_V)\) on the left of the target into the composite leg \(D(j\mathbin{;}\iota_U)\)
  via L2 (\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso_pullbackcongr}). Combining the \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorCongr}\,hjι\)
  factor that L2 produces with the \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,j\,\iota_U\) head of L1 reconstitutes
  \(\varrho = \mathtt{restrictCompReindex}\,j\), which is by definition
  \((\mathtt{restrictFunctorCongr}\,hjι)^{-1} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,j\,\iota_U\)
  (\cref{def:restrictcompreindex}), giving the head \(\varrho_{\mathtt{dual}\,M}\). On the dual flank the
  two contravariant \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\) transports fuse, by contravariant functoriality
  (\cref{lem:dualisoofiso_trans}, \cref{def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}), into the single factor
  \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,\varrho_M\). This gives the displayed square. The trivialisation
  step's \((\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,e^M)^{-1}\) transport and the datum refinement are not part of this
  square; they telescope in the parent (\cref{lem:trivialisation_restrict_compat}).
nodes.lean_source:
private lemma dual_restrict_iso_restrict_compat {X : Scheme.{u}} {U V : X.Opens}
    (j : (V : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme)) [IsOpenImmersion j] (hjι : j ≫ U.ι = V.ι)
    (M : X.Modules) :
    dual_restrict_iso V.ι M
      = restrictCompReindex j hjι (dual M)
          ≪≫ (restrictFunctor j).mapIso (dual_restrict_iso U.ι M)
          ≪≫ dual_restrict_iso j (M.restrict U.ι)
          ≪≫ dualIsoOfIso (restrictCompReindex j hjι M) := by

  have hL2 := dual_restrict_iso_pullbackCongr (f := j ≫ U.ι) (g := V.ι) hjι M
  have hVeq : dual_restrict_iso V.ι M
      = ((restrictFunctorCongr hjι).app M.dual).symm
          ≪≫ dual_restrict_iso (j ≫ U.ι) M
          ≪≫ dualIsoOfIso ((restrictFunctorCongr hjι).app M).symm := by
    rw [← hL2, ← Iso.trans_assoc, Iso.symm_self_id, Iso.refl_trans]

  rw [hVeq, dual_restrict_iso_comp j U.ι M]
  unfold restrictCompReindex
  rw [dualIsoOfIso_trans]

  simp only [Iso.trans_assoc]
  rfl

nodes.title: Oplax-monoidal unit map $\eta$ of the presheaf pullback --- conceptual anchor
nodes.statement:
\textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  For any oplax monoidal functor \(F\), Mathlib supplies the \emph{unit comparator}
  \(\eta_F : F(\mathbf 1) \to \mathbf 1\), the oplax counterpart of the unit map. Applied to the
  oplax-monoidal presheaf pullback \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi\)
  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}) along the chart immersion
  \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\), \(\varphi = j.\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom}\), it furnishes the bare
  oplax unit comparison
  \[
    \eta(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi) :
      (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf 1_U \longrightarrow \mathbf 1_V .
  \]
  This is the \emph{conceptual} pullback-flank unit map. Its invertibility is \emph{not} asserted
  here. The actual pullback-side comparator \(p\) used in the dual base-change theory is built
  instead as
  the H1 transport of the pushforward comparator \(q\); see
  \cref{def:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso}.

ok
[Pullback-side unit comparator $p$ --- the H1 realization]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) be the chart immersion, \(\varphi = j.\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom}\)
  the pullback ring map, and \(\beta\) the corresponding (sectionwise-bijective) pushforward ring
  map. The pullback-side unit comparator used throughout the dual base-change theory is the
  isomorphism
  \[
    p := \mathtt{presheafPullbackUnitIso}\,j :
      (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf 1_U \;\cong\; \mathbf 1_V ,
  \]
  realized \emph{not} as the bare oplax \(\eta\) (\cref{def:presheaf_pullback_unit_eta}) but through
  the \emph{H1 reconciliation}. Both \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) and the open-immersion
  pushforward \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\) are left adjoints of the same right adjoint
  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\); the left-adjoint-uniqueness isomorphism
  \[
    H_1 := \mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\bigl(\mathtt{pushforwardPushforwardAdj},\,
      \mathtt{pullbackPushforwardAdjunction}\,\varphi\bigr) :
      \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi
  \]
  identifies the two left adjoints (\cref{lem:unit_leftadjointuniq_hom_app},
  \cref{lem:pushforwardpushforwardadj_unit_app_app_apply}). Evaluated at the structure-sheaf unit
  \(\mathbf 1_U\), it carries the pullback flank to the pushforward flank, on which the pushforward
  comparator \(q = \mathtt{presheafPushforwardUnitIso}\,j\) (\cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso})
  contracts to \(\mathbf 1_V\). Composing,
  \[
    p \;=\; (H_1.\mathrm{app}\,\mathbf 1_U)^{-1} \,\mathbin{;}\, q ,
  \]
  the H1-transport of \(q\). This is exactly the identification by which the two flank comparators
  are matched at the structure-sheaf unit in the \(\theta\)-at-unit bridge
  \cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_pullback_natural}.
nodes.lean_source:
noncomputable def presheafPullbackUnitIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f] :
    letI φR : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
        (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
      (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom
    (PresheafOfModules.pullback φR).obj
        (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
      ≅ 𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) := by
  letI φR : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
    (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom
  letI α : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
    { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv, naturality := fun _ _ i => f.appIso_inv_naturality i }
  letI β : (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
      f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
    Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
  have hadj : PresheafOfModules.pushforward β ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward φR :=
    PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction β φR
      (by ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x))
      (by ext U x; exact congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x))
  letI H1 := hadj.leftAdjointUniq (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φR)
  exact (H1.app (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u}
      (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).symm ≪≫ presheafPushforwardUnitIso f

nodes.title: Lax-monoidal unit map $\varepsilon$ of the presheaf pushforward --- conceptual anchor
nodes.statement:
\textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  For any lax monoidal functor \(G\), Mathlib supplies the \emph{unit comparator}
  \(\varepsilon_G : \mathbf 1 \to G(\mathbf 1)\). Applied to the lax-monoidal presheaf pushforward
  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pushforward}\,\beta\) (\cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_laxmonoidal}) along
  the chart immersion, it gives the bare lax unit comparison
  \[
    \varepsilon(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta) :
      \mathbf 1_V \longrightarrow (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf 1_U ,
  \]
  the presheaf analogue of \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit}\). This is the
  \emph{conceptual} pushforward-flank unit map; its invertibility is supplied not by the lax
  structure but by the strong monoidality of \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\) for an open immersion
  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_beta_monoidal}), and the resulting comparator \(q\) is recorded in
  \cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso}.

ok
[Strong monoidality of the open-immersion presheaf pushforward $\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta$]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(f : Y \to X\) be an open immersion and \(\beta = \mathtt{whiskerRight}\,\alpha\,
  (\mathtt{forget}_2)\) the induced structure-ring comparison, where
  \(\alpha.\mathrm{app}\,U = (f.\mathtt{appIso}\,U)^{-1}\). Since \(f\) is an open immersion each
  \(\alpha.\mathrm{app}\,U\) is invertible, hence \(\beta\) is sectionwise bijective. Consequently the
  presheaf pushforward factors as the strong-monoidal composite
  \[
    \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \;=\;
      \mathtt{pushforward}_0^{\mathrm{Comm}} \,\circ\, \mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\beta ,
  \]
  in which the topological pushforward \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0^{\mathrm{Comm}}\) is strong monoidal
  and \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\beta\) is strong monoidal because \(\beta\) is sectionwise
  bijective (\(\mathtt{restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective}\)). Hence \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\)
  carries a \emph{strong}-monoidal structure, upgrading the ambient lax instance
  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_laxmonoidal}); in particular its unit comparator
  \(\varepsilon\) and tensorator \(\mu\) are isomorphisms.
nodes.lean_source:
@[implicit_reducible]
noncomputable def presheafPushforwardBetaMonoidal {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
    [IsOpenImmersion f] :
    letI α : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
      { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv, naturality := fun _ _ i => f.appIso_inv_naturality i }
    letI β : (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
        f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
      Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).Monoidal := by
  letI α : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
    { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv, naturality := fun _ _ i => f.appIso_inv_naturality i }
  letI β : (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
      f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
    Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
  have hβbij : ∀ U, Function.Bijective
      ((Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).app U).hom := by
    intro U
    haveI : IsIso (α.app U) := inferInstanceAs (IsIso (f.appIso U.unop).inv)
    have : IsIso ((Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).app U) := by
      rw [Functor.whiskerRight_app]
      exact Functor.map_isIso (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) (α.app U)
    exact ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso _
  letI mβ : (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars
      (Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).Monoidal :=
    PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective _ hβbij
  exact (inferInstance : (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀OfCommRingCat f.opensFunctor X.presheaf ⋙
    PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars
      (Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).Monoidal)

nodes.statement:
ok
[Pushforward-side unit comparator $q$ --- the strong-monoidal $\varepsilon$ inverse]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  In the open-immersion setting above, the strong monoidality of \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\)
  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_beta_monoidal}) makes the unit comparator an isomorphism
  \(\varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta) :
    \mathbf 1_V \cong (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf 1_U\). The pushforward-side
  unit comparator is its inverse,
  \[
    q := \varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta)^{-1} :
      (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf 1_U \;\cong\; \mathbf 1_V ,
  \]
  the strong-monoidal \(\varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}\) inverse (not the bare lax \(\varepsilon\) of
  \cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_eps}).
nodes.lean_source:
noncomputable def presheafPushforwardUnitIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f] :
    letI α : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
      { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv, naturality := fun _ _ i => f.appIso_inv_naturality i }
    letI β : (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
        f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
      Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).obj
        (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
      ≅ 𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) := by
  letI α : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
    { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv, naturality := fun _ _ i => f.appIso_inv_naturality i }
  letI β : (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
      f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
    Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
  letI _hm : (PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).Monoidal := presheafPushforwardBetaMonoidal f
  exact (Functor.Monoidal.εIso (PresheafOfModules.pushforward β)).symm

nodes.statement:
ok
[Carrier-value of the pushforward comparator: $q$ is the $f.\mathtt{appIso}$-conjugation on carriers]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
