  This is the unit-side analogue of the tensorator collapse
  \cref{lem:pushforward_mu_appiso_collapse}. With \(f : Y \to X\) an open immersion,
  \(\beta\) the (sectionwise-bijective) structure-ring comparison, and
  \(q = \mathtt{presheafPushforwardUnitIso}\,f = \varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}(\mathtt{pushforward}\,
  \beta)^{-1}\) (\cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso}), the sectionwise carrier action of
  \(q.\mathrm{hom} = \varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta)^{-1}\) at an
  \emph{arbitrary} carrier element \(y\) is, over a section \(V\),
  \[
    \bigl(q.\mathrm{hom}\bigr)_V (y)
      \;=\; (f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V).\mathrm{hom}\,(y) ,
  \]
  the \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\)-conjugation of \(y\). The unit case \(y = 1\) is the motivating
  instance; the general form is the one the L1\('\) consumer
  \cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_pullback_natural} requires (it instantiates at a non-unit
  carrier).
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{lem:presheaf_pushforward_beta_monoidal, def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso, lem:pushforward_mu_appiso_collapse, lem:restrictscalars_oplaxmonoidal_eta_app_one}
  \leanok
  We compute the carrier action of \(q.\mathrm{hom} = \varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}(\mathtt{pushforward}\,
  \beta)^{-1}\) at an arbitrary carrier element \(y\) by splitting the strong-monoidal structure of
  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\) along the factorization
  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta = \mathtt{pushforward}_0^{\mathrm{Comm}} \,\circ\,
  \mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\beta\) furnished by
  \cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_beta_monoidal}. The argument is cleanest run on the \emph{inverse}
  \(q.\mathrm{inv} = \varepsilon(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta)\): under \(\mathtt{Functor.Monoidal.comp}\)
  the lax unit comparator \(\varepsilon\) of the composite is the composite of the two factors'
  \(\varepsilon\)'s, and the scalar-forgetting pushforward \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0^{\mathrm{Comm}}\)
  has \(\varepsilon = \mathrm{id}\), so \(q.\mathrm{inv}\) reduces to the \(\varepsilon\)-value of
  \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\beta\). That value is the sectionwise structure-ring transport
  \((f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V).\mathrm{inv}\), by the abstract \(\varepsilon\)-template
  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal_eps_app} (the strong-monoidal companion of the oplax
  \(\eta\)-template \cref{lem:restrictscalars_oplaxmonoidal_eta_app_one}; \(\varepsilon = \eta^{-1}\)
  since \(\beta\) is sectionwise bijective). Hence
  \(\bigl(q.\mathrm{inv}\bigr)_V (w) = (f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V).\mathrm{inv}\,(w)\)
  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso_inv_app}). Now both \(\bigl(q.\mathrm{hom}\bigr)_V(y)\) and
  \((f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V).\mathrm{hom}\,(y)\) are sent to \(y\) by the injective
  \(\bigl(q.\mathrm{inv}\bigr)_V\) — the former by the round-trip \(q.\mathrm{hom}\,;q.\mathrm{inv} =
  \mathrm{id}\), the latter by \(\mathtt{appIso}\)'s hom-inv identity — so they are equal:
  \(\bigl(q.\mathrm{hom}\bigr)_V (y) = (f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V).\mathrm{hom}\,(y)\), the
  \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\)-conjugation of \(y\). This isolates the same structure-ring transport that the
  tensorator collapse \cref{lem:pushforward_mu_appiso_collapse} isolates on the \(\mu\)-side.
nodes.lean_source:
lemma pushforwardBetaUnitEpsAppOne {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y)ᵒᵖ)
    (y : letI α : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
           { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv, naturality := fun _ _ i => f.appIso_inv_naturality i }
         letI β : (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
             f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
           Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
         (((PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).obj
             (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u}
               (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).obj V : Type u)) :
    ((presheafPushforwardUnitIso f).hom.app V).hom y
      = (f.appIso V.unop).hom.hom y := by
  letI α : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
    { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv, naturality := fun _ _ i => f.appIso_inv_naturality i }
  letI β : (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
      f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
    Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
  letI _hm : (PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).Monoidal := presheafPushforwardBetaMonoidal f

  have hεval := presheafPushforwardUnitIso_inv_app f V

  have hinj : Function.Injective ((presheafPushforwardUnitIso f).inv.app V).hom := by
    apply Function.LeftInverse.injective
      (g := ((presheafPushforwardUnitIso f).hom.app V).hom)
    intro x
    rw [← LinearMap.comp_apply, ← ModuleCat.hom_comp, ← PresheafOfModules.comp_app,
      (presheafPushforwardUnitIso f).inv_hom_id]
    rfl
  apply hinj
  rw [hεval ((f.appIso V.unop).hom.hom y)]
  rw [← LinearMap.comp_apply, ← ModuleCat.hom_comp, ← PresheafOfModules.comp_app,
    (presheafPushforwardUnitIso f).hom_inv_id]
  rw [show ((f.appIso V.unop).inv.hom) ((f.appIso V.unop).hom.hom y) = y from
    ConcreteCategory.congr_hom (f.appIso V.unop).hom_inv_id y]
  rfl

nodes.statement:
ok
[Abstract $\varepsilon$-value of $\mathtt{restrictScalars}$ on carriers]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  For ring-presheaves \(R, S : \mathcal C^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathtt{CommRingCat}\) and a natural
  transformation \(\alpha : R \to S\), the lax unit comparator \(\varepsilon\) of
  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars}\,\alpha\) acts sectionwise on carriers by the ring map
  \(\alpha\): over a section \(W\), \(\varepsilon.\mathrm{app}\,W\,(r) = (\alpha.\mathrm{app}\,W).\mathrm{hom}\,(r)\).
  This is the abstract \(\{R,S : \mathcal C^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathtt{CommRingCat}\}\) companion of the
  unit-value template \cref{lem:restrictscalars_oplaxmonoidal_eta_app_one}; the abstract framing makes
  the ambient \(\mathtt{CommRing}\) instance native (the concrete post-\(\mathtt{forget}_2\) Scheme
  carrier exposes only \(\mathtt{Ring}\)).
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{lem:restrictscalars_oplaxmonoidal_eta_app_one}
  \leanok
  Unfold \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\)'s lax-monoidal \(\varepsilon\): it is, by construction,
  the scalar-action map induced by \(\alpha.\mathrm{app}\,W\) on the unit module, i.e. multiplication by
  \((\alpha.\mathrm{app}\,W).\mathrm{hom}\,(r)\). Reading off its value at a carrier element gives the
  claim directly.
nodes.lean_source:
lemma restrictScalars_laxMonoidal_ε_app {C : Type u} [Category.{u} C]
    {R S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) (W : Cᵒᵖ)
    (r : ((R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj W : Type u)) :
    ((Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α)).app W).hom r
      = (α.app W).hom r := by
  erw [ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in

nodes.statement:
ok
[Carrier-value of the pushforward comparator inverse $q.\mathrm{inv} = \varepsilon$]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  In the open-immersion setting, with \(q = \mathtt{presheafPushforwardUnitIso}\,f\)
  (\cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso}), the inverse \(q.\mathrm{inv} =
  \varepsilon(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta)\) acts sectionwise on carriers as the inverse structure-ring
  transport: over a section \(V\),
  \(\bigl(q.\mathrm{inv}\bigr)_V (w) = (f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V).\mathrm{inv}\,(w)\).
  This is the \(\varepsilon\)-side companion of \cref{lem:pushforward_beta_unit_eps_app_one}; it is
  consumed both inside that lemma and directly by the L1\('\) flank
  \cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_pullback_natural}.
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{lem:presheaf_pushforward_beta_monoidal, lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal_eps_app}
  \leanok
  Split \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta = \mathtt{pushforward}_0^{\mathrm{Comm}} \circ
  \mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\beta\) (\cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_beta_monoidal}). Under
  \(\mathtt{Functor.Monoidal.comp}\) the composite \(\varepsilon\) is the composite of the factors';
  \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0^{\mathrm{Comm}}\) contributes \(\varepsilon = \mathrm{id}\), leaving the
  \(\varepsilon\)-value of \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\beta\), which by
  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal_eps_app} is \((\beta.\mathrm{app}\,V)\)-multiplication, i.e.
  \((f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V).\mathrm{inv}\) on carriers. Hence
  \(\bigl(q.\mathrm{inv}\bigr)_V (w) = (f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V).\mathrm{inv}\,(w)\).
nodes.lean_source:
lemma presheafPushforwardUnitIso_inv_app {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y)ᵒᵖ)
    (w : ((𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u}
        (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).obj V : Type u)) :
    letI α : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
      { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv, naturality := fun _ _ i => f.appIso_inv_naturality i }
    letI β : (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
        f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
      Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
    ((presheafPushforwardUnitIso f).inv.app V).hom w
      = (f.appIso V.unop).inv.hom w := by
  letI α : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
    { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv, naturality := fun _ _ i => f.appIso_inv_naturality i }
  letI β : (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
      f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
    Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
  letI _hm : (PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).Monoidal := presheafPushforwardBetaMonoidal f
  have hq : (presheafPushforwardUnitIso f).inv
      = Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (PresheafOfModules.pushforward β) := rfl
  rw [hq]
  show (ModuleCat.Hom.hom ((Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε
      (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀OfCommRingCat f.opensFunctor X.presheaf ⋙
        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars
          (Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).app V)) w
    = (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (f.appIso V.unop).inv) w
  erw [Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp_ε]
  rw [PresheafOfModules.comp_app, ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply]
  have hmapid :
      (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map
          (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε
            (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀OfCommRingCat f.opensFunctor X.presheaf))
        = 𝟙 _ := CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id _ _
  rw [hmapid]
  erw [restrictScalars_laxMonoidal_ε_app (Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) V w]
  rfl

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in

nodes.statement:
ok
[S4a / L1$'$: $\theta$-at-unit bridge --- the presheaf dual base-change comparison at the structure sheaf is the dual-of-unit iso]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) be the chart immersion with \(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\),
  \(\varphi = j.\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom}\) the pullback ring map, and \(\beta\) the corresponding
  pushforward ring map. Evaluate the presheaf dual base-change comparison
  \(\theta_M = \mathtt{presheafDualPullbackComparison}\,j\,M\)
  (\cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison}) at the structure-sheaf unit
  \(M = \mathbf 1_U = \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,U.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\). Because \(\theta\) lands
  the dual on the pushforward flank, this gives
  \[
    \theta_{\mathbf 1} : \mathtt{pullback}_\varphi(\mathtt{dual}\,\mathbf 1_U)
      \;\cong\; \mathtt{dual}\bigl((\mathtt{pushforward}_\beta)\,\mathbf 1_U\bigr).
  \]
  Two \emph{distinct} unit comparators enter, one per flank:
  \begin{itemize}
    \item the pullback-side comparator
      \(p := \mathtt{presheafPullbackUnitIso}\,j : \mathtt{pullback}_\varphi\,\mathbf 1_U \cong \mathbf 1_V\)
      (\cref{def:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso}), the H1-transport \((H_1.\mathrm{app}\,\mathbf 1_U)^{-1}
      \mathbin{;} q\) of the pushforward comparator;
    \item the pushforward-side comparator
      \(q := \varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta)^{-1} :
        (\mathtt{pushforward}_\beta)\,\mathbf 1_U \cong \mathbf 1_V\)
      (\cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso}), the inverse of the strong-monoidal unit comparator
      of the pushforward.
  \end{itemize}
  Modulo the eval-at-\(1\) dual-of-unit isomorphism \(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}\)
  (\cref{def:presheaf_dual_unit_iso}) on the \(U\)- and \(V\)-flanks, \(\theta_{\mathbf 1}\)
  \emph{is} the dual-of-unit identification, with the two comparators on opposite flanks: \(q\) is
  transported contravariantly through \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\) onto the pushforward codomain of
  \(\theta_{\mathbf 1}\), while \(p\) closes the pullback domain on the right:
  \[
    \theta_{\mathbf 1}
      \,\mathbin{;}\, \bigl(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,q\bigr)^{-1}
      \,\mathbin{;}\, \mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}_V
    \;=\; \mathtt{pullback}_\varphi\bigl(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}_U\bigr)
      \,\mathbin{;}\, p,
  \]
  both sides being isomorphisms
  \(\mathtt{pullback}_\varphi(\mathtt{dual}\,\mathbf 1_U) \cong \mathbf 1_V\). This is the
  unit-specialisation of the immersion-naturality \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural}
  and the immersion analogue of the unit-automorphism core
  \cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_naturality}.
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison, lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural, lem:presheafdualunitiso_naturality, lem:internal_hom_eval, def:presheaf_dual_unit_iso, def:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso, def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso, lem:pushforward_beta_unit_eps_app_one, lem:slice_dual_transport_app_apply, lem:unit_leftadjointuniq_hom_app}
  \leanok
  By element extensionality on dual sections, exactly as in
  \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural}, of which this is the unit specialisation. Both
  \(\theta_{\mathbf 1}\) and \(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}\) are morphisms of presheaves of modules:
  \(\theta_{\mathbf 1}\) is the internal-hom base-change comparison
  \(f^{*}\mathcal{H}om(\mathbf 1, \mathcal{O}) \to \mathcal{H}om(f^{*}\mathbf 1, f^{*}\mathcal{O})\)
  (\cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison}), and \(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}\) is the
  sectionwise evaluation-at-\(1\) of the internal hom of the unit into itself
  (\cref{def:presheaf_dual_unit_iso}). Testing the displayed equality against an arbitrary dual
  section reduces, as in \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural}, to the built-in
  naturality of \(\theta\) (its \(\mathtt{.hom}\) naturality), now read through the eval-at-\(1\)
  identification, where the internal-hom evaluation (\cref{lem:internal_hom_eval}) collapses both
  composites to evaluation of the pulled-back section at the image of \(1\).

  The two flanks carry the two \emph{different} comparators. On the pushforward codomain of
  \(\theta_{\mathbf 1}\) the comparator \(q = \varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}(\mathtt{pushforward}\,
  \beta)^{-1}\) (\cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso}) is transported contravariantly by
  \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\); on the pullback domain the comparator
  \(p = \mathtt{presheafPullbackUnitIso}\,j\) (\cref{def:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso}) closes the
  square. Because \(p\) is, by definition, the H1-transport \((H_1.\mathrm{app}\,\mathbf 1_U)^{-1}
  \mathbin{;} q\), the two leading H1 factors --- the one hidden inside \(p\) and the one produced by
  unfolding \(\theta_{\mathbf 1}\) at the dual unit --- are telescoped away by the naturality of the
  natural isomorphism \(H_1 : \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathtt{pullback}\,
  \varphi\) at the dual-unit contraction \(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}_U\)
  (\cref{lem:unit_leftadjointuniq_hom_app}). After this cancellation the entire claim reduces to a
  single \emph{pushforward-flank} sectionwise identity ($\dagger$): writing \(\mathtt{sDT}\) for the
  slice-dual transport \(\mathtt{sliceDualTransport}\,f\,\mathbf 1_U\),
  \[
    \mathtt{sDT}
      \,\mathbin{;}\, \bigl(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,q\bigr)^{-1}
      \,\mathbin{;}\, \mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}_V
    \;=\;
    (\mathtt{pushforward}_\beta)\bigl(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}_U\bigr)
      \,\mathbin{;}\, q .
  \]

  This identity is proved sectionwise, as the immersion analogue of
  \cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_naturality}. Fix a section \(V\) and a dual section \(\varphi\).
  \begin{itemize}
    \item \emph{Left-hand side.} The verified forward transport formula
      (\cref{lem:slice_dual_transport_app_apply}) evaluates \(\mathtt{sDT}\) at the terminal slice as
      the reindexing across \(f.\mathtt{opensFunctor}\) conjugated by \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\) and the
      dual-unit ring swap; precomposing by \(q\) and evaluating at \(1\) (the dual-unit iso
      \(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso} = \mathtt{evalLin}\,(\cdot)\,1\),
      \cref{def:presheaf_dual_unit_iso}) exposes \(\mathtt{evalLin}\,\mathbf 1_U\,(\mathrm{op}\,fV)\,
      \varphi\,1\) conjugated by \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\) and the dual-unit ring swap.
    \item \emph{Right-hand side.} The pushforward of the eval-at-\(1\) contraction
      \((\mathtt{pushforward}_\beta)(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}_U)\) followed by the unit comparator
      \(q.\mathrm{hom}\) is reduced by the unit-value sub-lemma
      \cref{lem:pushforward_beta_unit_eps_app_one}: the carrier action of \(q.\mathrm{hom}\) at \(1\)
      is the \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\)-conjugation of \(1\), so the right side reduces to the same
      \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\)-conjugated \(\mathtt{evalLin}\,\mathbf 1_U\,(\mathrm{op}\,fV)\,\varphi\,1\).
  \end{itemize}
  Both sides being the same eval-at-\(1\) value conjugated by \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\), equality is the
  commutativity of \(\mathcal{O}_V\)-linear endomorphisms of the (commutative) structure ring --- the
  same \(\mathtt{linearEndo\_apply\_comm}\)-style step that closes
  \cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_naturality}, now with \(p\) and \(q\) in place of the unit
  automorphism. Hence both sides of ($\dagger$) agree sectionwise, and with the H1 telescope this gives the displayed
  square.
nodes.lean_source:
lemma presheafDualUnitIso_pullback_natural {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f] :
    letI φR : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
        (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
      (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom
    presheafDualPullbackComparison f (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
        ≪≫ (PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso (presheafPushforwardUnitIso f)).symm
        ≪≫ presheafDualUnitIso (Y := Y)
      = (PresheafOfModules.pullback φR).mapIso (presheafDualUnitIso (Y := X))
          ≪≫ presheafPullbackUnitIso f := by

  letI φR : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
    (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom
  letI α : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
    { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv, naturality := fun _ _ i => f.appIso_inv_naturality i }
  letI β : (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
      f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
    Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)

  letI hadj : PresheafOfModules.pushforward β ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward φR :=
    PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction β φR
      (by ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x))
      (by ext U x; exact congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x))
  letI H1 := hadj.leftAdjointUniq (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φR)

  have FLANK :
      (PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun V => sliceDualTransport f (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) V)
            (by intro V W g; subsingleton)).hom ≫
          (PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso (presheafPushforwardUnitIso f)).inv
            ≫ (presheafDualUnitIso (Y := Y)).hom
        = (PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).map (presheafDualUnitIso (Y := X)).hom
            ≫ (presheafPushforwardUnitIso f).hom := by

    apply PresheafOfModules.hom_ext
    intro V
    apply ModuleCat.hom_ext
    apply LinearMap.ext
    intro φ

    simp only [PresheafOfModules.comp_app, ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply,
      PresheafOfModules.isoMk_hom_app]
    erw [sliceDualTransport_app_apply f (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) V φ]

    rw [dualUnitRingSwap_apply]
    erw [pushforwardBetaUnitEpsAppOne f V
      ((((PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).map (presheafDualUnitIso (Y := X)).hom).app V).hom φ)]
    congr 1
    erw [PresheafOfModules.pushforward_map_app_apply]

    erw [presheafPushforwardUnitIso_inv_app f (Opposite.op (Opposite.unop V))]
    erw [map_one]
    rfl
  apply Iso.ext

  change ((H1.app ((𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u}
              (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).dual)).inv ≫
        (PresheafOfModules.isoMk
          (fun V => sliceDualTransport f (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) V)
          (by intro V W g; subsingleton)).hom) ≫
        (PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso (presheafPushforwardUnitIso f)).inv
          ≫ (presheafDualUnitIso (Y := Y)).hom
      = (PresheafOfModules.pullback φR).map (presheafDualUnitIso (Y := X)).hom ≫
        (H1.app (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u}
            (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).inv
          ≫ (presheafPushforwardUnitIso f).hom

  have hnat :
      (PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).map (presheafDualUnitIso (Y := X)).hom ≫
          (H1.app (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u}
            (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).hom
        = (H1.app ((𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u}
              (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).dual)).hom ≫
            (PresheafOfModules.pullback φR).map (presheafDualUnitIso (Y := X)).hom :=
    H1.hom.naturality (presheafDualUnitIso (Y := X)).hom

  rw [Category.assoc, Iso.inv_comp_eq,
    ← Category.assoc (H1.app ((𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u}
        (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).dual)).hom
      ((PresheafOfModules.pullback φR).map (presheafDualUnitIso (Y := X)).hom),
    ← hnat, Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
  exact FLANK

nodes.statement:
ok
[S4a / L2$'$: unit assembly --- the dual-restriction comparison at the unit intertwines the dual-unit contractions]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  With \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) as above, write \(\mathbf 1_U, \mathbf 1_V\) for the structure
  sheaves, \(u_j = \mathtt{unitRestrictIso}\,j\) (\cref{def:unitrestrictiso}) for the chart-scheme
  unit identification \(\mathbf 1_U|_j \cong \mathbf 1_V\), and
  \(\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_U, \mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_V\) for the dual-unit contractions
  (\cref{lem:dual_unit_iso}). The \emph{bare} dual-restriction comparison
  \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,j\,(\mathbf 1_U) : (\mathtt{dual}\,\mathbf 1_U)|_j \cong
  \mathtt{dual}(\mathbf 1_U|_j)\) (\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso}) intertwines the two contractions
  with the unit-restriction identification:
  \[
    (\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_U \,\mathbin{;}\, u_j
      \;=\; \mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,j\,(\mathbf 1_U)
        \,\mathbin{;}\, \bigl(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,u_j\bigr)^{-1}
        \,\mathbin{;}\, \mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_V,
  \]
  both sides being isomorphisms \((\mathtt{dual}\,\mathbf 1_U)|_j \cong \mathbf 1_V\).
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{lem:presheafdualunitiso_pullback_natural, lem:dual_restrict_iso_eq_comparison, lem:sheafificationcomppullback_restrict, lem:dual_unit_iso, def:unitrestrictiso, def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso, lem:image_preimage_of_le, lem:forget_unit_restrict_eq_pushforward_unit, lem:pullback_unit_sheafify_reconcile}
  Drop both sides to their underlying morphisms (\(\mathtt{Iso.ext}\)) and flatten every
  \(\mathbin{;}\), \(\mathrm{mapIso}\) and inverse into a single right-associated composite. Expanding
  the bare comparison \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,j\,(\mathbf 1_U)\) by the dual-B1 factorisation
  (\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso_eq_comparison}) into the three-factor composite
  \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\,j \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\,\varphi
  \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sheafification}(\theta_{\mathbf 1})\), and each contraction by its definition
  \(\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso} = \mathtt{sheafification}(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}) \mathbin{;}
  \mathtt{counit}\) (\cref{lem:dual_unit_iso}), turns the square into a purely syntactic identity of
  two right-associated \(\mathbin{;}\)-chains.

  This flattened identity is discharged by a verified \emph{category-algebra assembly}: an abstract
  reassociation lemma over a bare category, proven by associativity bookkeeping alone, that crosses
  the \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,\mathbin{;}\) seam without any unfolding. The assembly splits the
  left-hand unit-restriction map \(m\) as a prefix \(m_{\mathrm p}\) followed by a core
  \(m_{\mathrm c}\) and consumes four hypotheses --- a split identity, a flank identity, an
  scp-prefix identity, and a tail identity. The split, scp-prefix and tail data are folded
  \(\mathbin{;}\)-bookkeeping (the \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback} \mathbin{;}
  \mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\) prefix is matched, exactly as in S3, by the composition law of
  bridge~(b), \cref{lem:sheafificationcomppullback_restrict}, threading the equality of opens
  \(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\), \cref{lem:image_preimage_of_le}; and the
  sheafification-counit naturality moves the counits past the restriction while contravariant
  functoriality of \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\), \cref{def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}, collects the
  dual transport into \((\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,u_j)^{-1}\)). The whole \(\theta\)-at-unit
  identification supplied by L1$'$ (\cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_pullback_natural}) is consumed
  inside this fold. The substantive content of the four hypotheses reduces to exactly two residual
  reconciliations:

  \begin{itemize}
    \item the \emph{dual flank} (\cref{lem:forget_unit_restrict_eq_pushforward_unit}): the
      forgotten sheaf-level unit-restriction comparator equals the presheaf pushforward-unit
      comparator \(q\), which discharges the dual-flank leg of the assembly;
    \item the \emph{pullback (unit-only) flank} (\cref{lem:pullback_unit_sheafify_reconcile}): the
      sheaf pullback-unit comparator sheafifies the presheaf pullback-unit comparator \(p\), threaded
      through \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\) and the two sheafification counits. After
      reduced-image/SCP-naturality and the common-prefix cancel, the residual is unit-only --- no
      dual factor remains.
  \end{itemize}

  Modulo these two reconciliations the square is closed by the folded assembly; the keystone is
  therefore sorry-free up to them. The genuine open work is precisely these two adjunction-coherence
  reconciliations, both of which consume the already-proven left-adjoint-uniqueness and
  hom-transpose infrastructure (\cref{lem:sheafification_comp_pullback_eq_leftadjointuniq},
  \cref{lem:leftadjointuniq_app_unit_eta_general}, \cref{lem:comp_homequiv_factor_sheafify_pullback},
  \cref{lem:unit_leftadjointuniq_hom_app}, \cref{lem:pushforward_beta_unit_eps_app_one}).
nodes.lean_source:
private lemma dual_unit_iso_restrict_assemble {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f] :
    (restrictFunctor f).mapIso (dual_unit_iso (Y := X))
        ≪≫ unitRestrictIso f
      = dual_restrict_iso f (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
          ≪≫ (dualIsoOfIso (unitRestrictIso f)).symm
          ≪≫ dual_unit_iso (Y := Y) := by

  apply Iso.ext
  simp only [Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom, Iso.symm_hom, Iso.app_hom, dual_unit_iso,
    unitRestrictIso, dual_restrict_iso_eq_comparison]

  have hL1'h := congrArg Iso.hom (presheafDualUnitIso_pullback_natural f)
  simp only [Iso.trans_hom, Iso.symm_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom] at hL1'h

  simp only [dualIsoOfIso, Functor.mapIso_inv]

  refine unit_assemble ((pullback f).map (dual_unit_iso (Y := X)).hom) ?hnat ?hcore
  · 
    exact (restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).hom.naturality (dual_unit_iso (Y := X)).hom
  · 

    have hsplit : (pullback f).map (dual_unit_iso (Y := X)).hom
        = (pullback f).map ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
              (presheafDualUnitIso (Y := X)).hom)
          ≫ (pullback f).map ((asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
              (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).hom.app (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)) :=
      Functor.map_comp (pullback f) _ _

    have hflank : (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
            (presheafDualPullbackComparison f (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).hom
          ≫ (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
              (PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).mapIso
                ((restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
                  ≪≫ pullbackUnitIso f))).inv
          ≫ (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
              (presheafDualUnitIso (Y := Y)).hom
        = (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
              ((PresheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).map
                (presheafDualUnitIso (Y := X)).hom)
          ≫ (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
              (presheafPullbackUnitIso f).hom := by
      rw [forgetUnitRestrict_eq_pushforwardUnit f, ← Functor.map_comp, ← Functor.map_comp]

      exact (congrArg (fun m => (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
        (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map m) hL1'h).trans (Functor.map_comp _ _ _)

    have hscp := ((SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom.naturality
      (presheafDualUnitIso (Y := X)).hom).symm
    exact hcore_assemble hsplit hflank hscp (pullbackUnit_sheafify_reconcile f)

nodes.statement:
ok
[RES1 (dual-flank reconciliation): the forgotten sheaf unit-restriction iso equals the presheaf
pushforward-unit comparator]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(f : Y \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion with pullback ring map
  \(\varphi = f.\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom}\) and pushforward ring map \(\beta\). Applying the
  forgetful functor \(\mathtt{forget} : \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,Y \to \mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\)
  to the composite of the chart-scheme unit identification
  \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\,f\) (\cref{def:restrictfunctorisopullback}) at the
  structure-sheaf unit \(\mathbf 1_X\) with the sheaf pullback-unit iso \(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f\)
  yields, on the nose, the presheaf pushforward-unit comparator
  \(q = \mathtt{presheafPushforwardUnitIso}\,f\) (\cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso}, the inverse
  strong-monoidal unit comparator \(\varepsilon(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta)^{-1}\)):
  \[
    \mathtt{forget}.\mathrm{mapIso}\bigl(
      (\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\,f).\mathrm{app}\,(\mathbf 1_X)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f\bigr)
      \;=\; \mathtt{presheafPushforwardUnitIso}\,f .
  \]
  This is the leg of the unit assembly (\cref{lem:dual_unit_iso_restrict_assemble}) that closes its
  dual flank.
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{def:restrictfunctorisopullback, lem:pushforward_beta_unit_eps_app_one, lem:unit_leftadjointuniq_hom_app, lem:homequiv_leftadjointuniq_hom_app, lem:leftadjointuniq_hom_app_counit, lem:presheafdualunitiso_naturality, def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso, lem:pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
  Reduce to a sectionwise carrier identity at an open \(V\) and a carrier element \(y\) (drop both
  isomorphisms to their homs, then test presheaf-of-modules morphisms section by section and the
  resulting \(\mathcal{O}_V\)-linear maps element by element).

  On the right, the carrier action of \(q.\mathrm{hom}\) at \(V\) is the structure-ring isomorphism
  \((f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V).\mathrm{hom}\) by the proven linchpin
  \cref{lem:pushforward_beta_unit_eps_app_one}.

  On the left, the sectionwise carrier is the composite of the two sheaf-level unit comparators
  \((\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\,f).\mathrm{app}\,\mathbf 1_X\) followed by
  \(\mathtt{pullbackObjUnitToUnit}\,\varphi\). The first comparator is the left-adjoint-uniqueness
  isomorphism \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}(\mathtt{restrictAdjunction}\,f)\,
  (\mathtt{pullbackPushforwardAdjunction}\,f)\) (\cref{def:restrictfunctorisopullback}); the second is
  the adjunction transpose \((\mathtt{pullbackPushforwardAdjunction}\,f).\mathrm{homEquiv}^{-1}\) of
  the canonical \(\mathtt{unitToPushforwardObjUnit}\) (\cref{lem:pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}).
  Collapsing the composite
  \[
    (\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\,\mathrm{adj}_1\,\mathrm{adj}_2).\mathrm{hom}.\mathrm{app}\,x
      \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathrm{adj}_2.\mathrm{homEquiv}^{-1}(g)
      \;=\; \mathrm{adj}_1.\mathrm{homEquiv}^{-1}(g)
  \]
  by the uniqueness coherences \cref{lem:homequiv_leftadjointuniq_hom_app},
  \cref{lem:leftadjointuniq_hom_app_counit} and the unit triangle
  \cref{lem:unit_leftadjointuniq_hom_app} rewrites the left composite as the single
  \(\mathtt{restrictAdjunction}\)-unit transpose at \(V\).
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  is again \((f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V).\mathrm{hom}\) --- the structure-sheaf specialisation, exactly
  mirroring the eval-core template \cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_naturality}. Both sides equal
  \((f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V).\mathrm{hom}\), closing the identity.
nodes.lean_source:
private lemma forgetUnitRestrict_eq_pushforwardUnit {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
    [IsOpenImmersion f] :
    (SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).mapIso
        ((restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
          ≪≫ pullbackUnitIso f)
      = presheafPushforwardUnitIso f := by

  have hcollapse :
      ((restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).hom
          ≫ (pullbackUnitIso f).hom
        = ((restrictAdjunction f).homEquiv _ _).symm
            (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) := by
    rw [show (pullbackUnitIso f).hom
          = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom from rfl,
      ← SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_symm_unitToPushforwardObjUnit,
      show ((restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).hom
          = ((restrictAdjunction f).leftAdjointUniq
              (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f)).hom.app
              (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) from rfl]
    exact leftAdjointUniq_hom_app_homEquiv_symm (restrictAdjunction f)
      (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f) _

  have hmor :
      ((restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).hom
          ≫ (pullbackUnitIso f).hom
        = (restrictFunctor f).map
              (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)
            ≫ (restrictAdjunction f).counit.app (SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf) :=
    hcollapse.trans (Adjunction.homEquiv_counit (restrictAdjunction f) _ _
      (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom))
  apply Iso.ext
  simp only [Functor.mapIso_hom, Iso.trans_hom]
  refine Eq.trans (congrArg (SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map hmor) ?_

  refine Eq.trans ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map_comp _ _) ?_

  apply PresheafOfModules.hom_ext
  intro V
  apply ModuleCat.hom_ext
  apply LinearMap.ext
  intro y
  refine Eq.trans ?_ (pushforwardBetaUnitEpsAppOne f V y).symm
  simp only [SheafOfModules.forget_map, PresheafOfModules.comp_app,
    ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply]

  erw [PresheafOfModules.pushforward_map_app_apply,
    SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit_val_app_apply]

  erw [SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj_counit_app_val_app]

  rw [Scheme.Hom.appIso_hom]

  have hops : (f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction).unit.app (Opposite.unop V)
      = eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq (Opposite.unop V)).symm := Subsingleton.elim _ _
  rw [hops, CommRingCat.comp_apply]
  rfl

nodes.statement:
ok
[RES2 L1: the unit pullback identification factors through \texttt{sheafificationCompPullback} and the
$X$-sheafification counit]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(f : Y \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion with pullback ring map
  \(\varphi = f.\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom}\), let \(M_0 = \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,\mathcal O_X\) be
  the structure-sheaf unit over \(X\) with underlying presheaf
  \(\mathbf 1^{\mathrm p}_X = M_0.\mathrm{val}\), and write
  \(\mathtt{cuX} = (\mathtt{sheafificationAdjunction}\,\mathbf 1_X).\mathrm{counit}\) for the
  $X$-sheafification counit. The per-object pullback identification
  \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,M_0\) (\cref{def:pullback_val_iso}) factors, on the nose, as the
  inverse \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\,\varphi\) comparison at \(\mathbf 1^{\mathrm p}_X\)
  followed by the sheaf pullback of \(\mathtt{cuX}\) at \(M_0\):
  \[
    (\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,M_0).\mathrm{hom}
      = (\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{inv}.\mathrm{app}\,\mathbf 1^{\mathrm p}_X
        \;\mathbin{;}\; (\mathtt{pullback}\,f).\mathrm{map}\bigl(\mathtt{cuX}.\mathrm{app}\,M_0\bigr).
  \]
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{def:pullback_val_iso}
  Immediate from the definition
  \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,M_0 =
  ((\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{app}\,\mathbf 1^{\mathrm p}_X)^{-1}
  \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{pullback}\,f).\mathrm{mapIso}\,((\mathtt{asIso}\,\mathtt{cuX}).\mathrm{app}\,M_0)\)
  (\cref{def:pullback_val_iso}): reading off the homs of the two composed isomorphisms gives the
  displayed identity by reflexivity. This is the unit instance of the template factorisation
  \(\mathtt{hpbv}\) of \cref{lem:sheaf_pullback_unit_forget_eq}.
nodes.lean_source:
private lemma pullbackValIso_unit_factor {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f] :
    (pullbackValIso f (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).hom
      = (SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).inv.app
            (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).val
        ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).map
            ((asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
              (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).hom.app (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)) := by
  rw [pullbackValIso, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.symm_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom]
  rfl

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in

nodes.statement:
ok
[RES2 L2: the sheafified presheaf pullback-unit comparator reconciles with the sheaf pullback
identification]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  With notation as above, let \(\mathtt{cuY} = (\mathtt{sheafificationAdjunction}\,\mathbf 1_Y).\mathrm{counit}\)
  be the $Y$-sheafification counit, \(a_Y = \mathtt{sheafification}\,\mathbf 1_Y\), and
  \(p = \mathtt{presheafPullbackUnitIso}\,f\) (\cref{def:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso}, the presheaf
  pullback-unit comparator \(p = (H_1\,\mathbf 1^{\mathrm p}_X)^{-1} \mathbin{;} q\) with \(H_1\) the
  \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) factor and \(q = \mathtt{presheafPushforwardUnitIso}\,f\),
  \cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso}). Then sheafifying \(p\) and cancelling the
  $Y$-sheafification counit reproduces the sheaf pullback identification followed by the sheaf
  pullback-unit iso \(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f\) (\cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso}):
  \[
    a_Y.\mathrm{map}\,(p.\mathrm{hom}) \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{cuY}.\mathrm{app}\,\mathbf 1_Y
      = (\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,M_0).\mathrm{hom}
        \;\mathbin{;}\; (\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f).\mathrm{hom}.
  \]
  Both sides are sheaf morphisms
  \(a_Y\bigl((\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi').\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf 1^{\mathrm p}_X\bigr) \to \mathcal O_Y\).
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{lem:sheaf_pullback_unit_forget_eq, def:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso, lem:sheafificationcomppullback_restrict, lem:leftadjointuniq_app_unit_eta_general, lem:unit_leftadjointuniq_hom_app}
  This is the structure-sheaf-unit specialisation of the proven template
  \cref{lem:sheaf_pullback_unit_forget_eq}, which already carries out the sheafification-intertwining
  mate-calculus that identifies the opaque sheaf pullback unit with its presheaf transpose. Unfold
  \(p = (H_1\,\mathbf 1^{\mathrm p}_X)^{-1} \mathbin{;} q\) (\cref{def:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso}). The
  \(H_1\) factor is the presheaf-level \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) comparison; transposing the
  template's conclusion at \(M_0\) and threading the \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) transport of the
  composite unit (\cref{lem:leftadjointuniq_app_unit_eta_general}) and the unit triangle
  \(\mathtt{unit\_leftAdjointUniq\_hom\_app}\) (\cref{lem:unit_leftadjointuniq_hom_app}) identifies the
  sheafified \(H_1\)/\(q\) presheaf comparator, after the \(\mathtt{cuY}\) cancellation, with the sheaf
  pullback identification \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,M_0\) followed by
  \(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f\). The reduced-image/restriction matching between the two
  \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\) presentations is supplied as in bridge~(b)
  (\cref{lem:sheafificationcomppullback_restrict}).
nodes.lean_source:
private lemma presheafPullbackUnitIso_sheafify_reconcile {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
    [IsOpenImmersion f] :
    (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
        (presheafPullbackUnitIso f).hom
      ≫ (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
          (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).hom.app (SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf)
      = (pullbackValIso f (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).hom ≫ (pullbackUnitIso f).hom := by

  have hp : (presheafPullbackUnitIso f).hom
      = (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
            (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).unit.app
          ((PresheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).obj
            (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).val)
        ≫ (SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map
            ((pullbackValIso f (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).hom
              ≫ (pullbackUnitIso f).hom) := by
    simp only [presheafPullbackUnitIso, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.symm_hom, Iso.app_inv]
    erw [H1inv_app_eq_pullbackVal_restrict f (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)]

    have hqid : (SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map
          ((restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).inv
        ≫ (presheafPushforwardUnitIso f).hom
      = (SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackUnitIso f).hom := by
      have hq : (presheafPushforwardUnitIso f).hom
          = (SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map
              ((restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).hom
            ≫ (SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackUnitIso f).hom := by
        rw [← forgetUnitRestrict_eq_pushforwardUnit f, Functor.mapIso_hom, Iso.trans_hom,
          Functor.map_comp]
      rw [hq, ← Category.assoc, ← Functor.mapIso_inv, ← Functor.mapIso_hom, Iso.inv_hom_id,
        Category.id_comp]
    rw [Category.assoc, Category.assoc, hqid, Functor.map_comp]

  rw [hp, asIso_hom]
  exact adj_unit_counit_collapse (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
    (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)) ((pullbackValIso f (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).hom
      ≫ (pullbackUnitIso f).hom)

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in

nodes.statement:
ok
[$(*)$ / RES2 (pullback unit-only reconciliation): the sheaf pullback-unit comparator sheafifies
the presheaf pullback-unit comparator]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(f : Y \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion. Pulling back the sheafification counit over
  \(X\) at the structure-sheaf unit and then applying the sheaf pullback-unit iso
  \(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f\) equals: the \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\) comparison at the
  unit presheaf, then the sheafification of the presheaf pullback-unit comparator
  \(p = \mathtt{presheafPullbackUnitIso}\,f\) (\cref{def:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso}), then the
  sheafification counit over \(Y\) at the unit:
  \[
    (\mathtt{pullback}\,f).\mathrm{map}\bigl(\mathtt{sheafCounit}_X.\mathrm{app}\,\mathbf 1_X\bigr)
      \;\mathbin{;}\; (\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f).\mathrm{hom}
      \;=\; \mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\,\varphi.\mathrm{hom}.\mathrm{app}\,\mathbf 1_X^{\mathrm p}
  \]
  \[
      \qquad\qquad
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{sheafification}.\mathrm{map}\bigl((\mathtt{presheafPullbackUnitIso}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{sheafCounit}_Y.\mathrm{app}\,\mathbf 1_Y ,
  \]
  where \(\mathtt{sheafCounit}_\bullet\) is the counit of the sheafification adjunction over
  \(\bullet\) and \(\mathbf 1_X^{\mathrm p}\) the underlying presheaf unit. This is the genuine
  unit-side reconciliation of the dual-unit naturality square; no dual factor remains.
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{lem:sheafification_comp_pullback_eq_leftadjointuniq, def:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso, lem:pullback_unit_iso, lem:pullback_val_iso_unit_factor, lem:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso_sheafify_reconcile}
  Rewrite the right-hand comparator leg
  \(a_Y.\mathrm{map}(p.\mathrm{hom}) \mathbin{;} \mathtt{cuY}.\mathrm{app}\,\mathbf 1_Y\) by L2
  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso_sheafify_reconcile}), then expand
  \((\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,M_0).\mathrm{hom}\) by L1
  (\cref{lem:pullback_val_iso_unit_factor}); the leading
  \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\,\varphi\) hom/inv legs cancel
  (\(\mathtt{Iso.hom\_inv\_id\_app}\)), leaving the left-hand side
  \((\mathtt{pullback}\,f).\mathrm{map}(\mathtt{cuX}.\mathrm{app}\,M_0) \mathbin{;}
  (\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\). All seam-crossing mate-calculus is confined to L2.
nodes.lean_source:
private lemma pullbackUnit_sheafify_reconcile {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f] :
    (pullback f).map
        ((asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).hom.app
          (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf))
        ≫ (pullbackUnitIso f).hom
      = (SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom.app
            (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).val
        ≫ (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
            (presheafPullbackUnitIso f).hom
        ≫ (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).hom.app
            (SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf) := by

  refine Eq.trans ?_ (congrArg
    ((SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom.app
      (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).val ≫ ·) (presheafPullbackUnitIso_sheafify_reconcile f)).symm
  rw [pullbackValIso_unit_factor f, Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_app_assoc]

nodes.statement:
ok
[S4a: dual-unit contraction commutes with further restriction]
  
  
  
  With \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) as above, the unit-duality contraction
  \(\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso} : \mathcal{H}om(\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{O}_U) \cong \mathcal{O}_U\)
  (\cref{lem:dual_unit_iso}) commutes with further restriction along \(j\). Writing
  \(\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_U\), \(\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_V\) for the contraction over \(U\), \(V\)
  and \(u_j = \mathtt{unitRestrictIso}\,j\) (\cref{def:unitrestrictiso}) for the chart-scheme unit
  identification, the compatibility is the conjugated four-leg identity
  \[
    \mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_V
      \;=\; \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,(u_j)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \bigl(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,j\,(\mathbf 1_U)\bigr)^{-1}
        \;\mathbin{;}\; (\mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,j).\mathrm{mapIso}\,\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_U
        \;\mathbin{;}\; u_j,
  \]
  where \(\mathbf 1_U\) is the structure-sheaf unit over \(U\), \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,j\)
  (\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso}) is the dual-restriction comparison along \(j\), and
  \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\) (\cref{def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}) is the contravariant transport
  of \(u_j\) on the dual flank.
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{lem:dual_unit_iso_restrict_assemble, lem:presheafdualunitiso_pullback_natural, lem:dual_unit_iso, def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso, def:unitrestrictiso}
  Pure folded iso-algebra from the unit assembly square L2$'$
  (\cref{lem:dual_unit_iso_restrict_assemble}), which records
  \[
    (\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_U \,\mathbin{;}\, u_j
      = \mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,j\,(\mathbf 1_U)
        \,\mathbin{;}\, \bigl(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,u_j\bigr)^{-1}
        \,\mathbin{;}\, \mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_V .
  \]
  Solving this square for \(\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_V\) --- cancelling the invertible factors
  \((\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,u_j)^{-1}\) and \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,j\,(\mathbf 1_U)\) on the
  right by the self-inverse cancellations of an isomorphism with its inverse ---
  yields the conjugated four-leg identity verbatim, with
  \((\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,j\,(\mathbf 1_U))^{-1}\) the middle leg and
  \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,u_j\), \(u_j\) the two outer transports. The seam-crossing
  \(\theta\)-at-unit identification is confined to L2$'$ (via L1$'$,
  \cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_pullback_natural}); no unfolding is needed here.
nodes.lean_source:
private lemma dual_unit_iso_restrict_compat {X : Scheme.{u}} {U V : X.Opens}
    (j : (V : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme)) [IsOpenImmersion j] (_hjι : j ≫ U.ι = V.ι) :
    dual_unit_iso (Y := (V : Scheme))
      = dualIsoOfIso (unitRestrictIso j)
          ≪≫ (dual_restrict_iso j (SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf)).symm
          ≪≫ (restrictFunctor j).mapIso (dual_unit_iso (Y := (U : Scheme)))
          ≪≫ unitRestrictIso j := by

  rw [dual_unit_iso_restrict_assemble j, Iso.symm_self_id_assoc, Iso.self_symm_id_assoc]

nodes.statement:
ok
[The unit contraction is the left unitor at the unit (Cone A coverage)]
  
  
  
  The structure-sheaf unit contraction
  \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso} : \mathcal{O}_X \otimes_X \mathcal{O}_X \cong \mathcal{O}_X\)
  coincides with the left unitor \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\) (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso})
  evaluated at the module argument \(M = \mathcal{O}_X\).
nodes.lean_source:
private lemma tensorObj_unit_iso_eq_left_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} :
    tensorObj_unit_iso (X := X)
      = tensorObj_left_unitor (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) := by
  unfold tensorObj_unit_iso tensorObj_left_unitor
  rfl

nodes.statement:
ok
[Naturality of the left unitor (Cone A coverage)]
  
  
  
  For a morphism \(g : M \to N\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, the left unitor
  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}) is natural:
  \[
    (\mathbf 1_{\mathcal{O}_X} \otimes g) \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\,N).\mathrm{hom}
      = (\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\,M).\mathrm{hom} \mathbin{;} g .
  \]
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
  The left unitor is the sheafification of the presheaf-level left unitor composed with the
  sheafification counit; both are natural transformations, so the square commutes by naturality of
  each leg.
nodes.lean_source:
private lemma tensorObj_left_unitor_naturality {W : Scheme.{u}} {M M' : W.Modules} (g : M ≅ M') :
    tensorObjIsoOfIso (Iso.refl (SheafOfModules.unit W.ringCatSheaf)) g
        ≪≫ tensorObj_left_unitor M'
      = tensorObj_left_unitor M ≪≫ g := by
  apply Iso.ext

  have hpre : MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
        (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (W.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
        (𝟙 ((SheafOfModules.forget W.ringCatSheaf).obj (SheafOfModules.unit W.ringCatSheaf)))
        ((SheafOfModules.forget W.ringCatSheaf).map g.hom)
      ≫ ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := W.presheaf)).leftUnitor M'.val).hom
      = ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := W.presheaf)).leftUnitor M.val).hom
        ≫ (SheafOfModules.forget W.ringCatSheaf).map g.hom := by
    exact MonoidalCategory.leftUnitor_naturality _
  simp only [tensorObjIsoOfIso, tensorObj_left_unitor, Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom,
    MonoidalCategory.tensorIso_hom, Functor.mapIso_refl, Iso.refl_hom, Category.assoc]

  rw [← Category.assoc]
  erw [← Functor.map_comp, hpre, Functor.map_comp, Category.assoc]
  erw [(PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 W.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit.naturality g.hom]
  rfl

nodes.statement:
ok
[$\eta$ mate-identification: the unit iso is the sheafified presheaf unit comparison (Cone A)]
  
  
  
  Let \(f : Y \to X\) be a morphism of schemes and \(\varphi'\) its associated change-of-rings map.
  The sheaf-level unit comparison \(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f\) (\cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso}) is the
  sheafification transport of the presheaf oplax unit comparison
  \(\eta\,(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi')\) (\cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}):
  \[
    (\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f).\mathrm{hom}
      = (\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,\mathcal{O}_X).\mathrm{inv}
        \mathbin{;} a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl(\eta\,(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi')\bigr)
        \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}.\mathrm{hom},
  \]
  where \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\) (\cref{def:pullback_val_iso}) reconciles the sheaf-level pullback
  value with the sheafified presheaf pullback and \(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}\) --- the unit-specific
  sheafification reconciliation isomorphism --- closes the unit (\(\mathbf 1\)) seam.
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{lem:pullback_unit_iso, lem:pullback_tensor_iso_unit, def:pullback_val_iso}
  This is the unit square already isolated in the proof that the tensor comparison on the unit pair is
  an isomorphism (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_unit}): there the right-hand composite is shown to
  equal \(\mathtt{pullbackObjUnitToUnit}\,\varphi\), which by definition is
  \(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f\) (\cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso}). Reading that square as a defining
  equation for the sheafified \(\eta\) yields the displayed identity.
nodes.lean_source:
private lemma pullbackUnitIso_eq_sheafify_eta {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) :
    letI φ' : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
        (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
        (f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom
    (pullbackUnitIso f).hom
      = (pullbackValIso f (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).inv ≫
          (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
            (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.η (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ')) ≫ sheafifyUnitIso.hom := by
  exact (pullbackEtaUnitSquare f).symm

nodes.statement:
ok
[$\delta$ mate-identification: the tensor comparison is the sheafified cotensorator (Cone A)]
  
  
  
  Let \(f : Y \to X\) and \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The sheaf-level tensor comparison
  \(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\,f\,M\,N\) (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map}) is the sheafification
  transport of the presheaf oplax cotensorator
  \(\delta\,(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi')\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\,N_{\mathrm{val}}\)
  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}):
  \[
    \mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\,f\,M\,N
      = (\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,(M \otimes_X N)).\mathrm{inv}
        \mathbin{;} a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl(\delta\,(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi')\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\,N_{\mathrm{val}}\bigr)
        \mathbin{;} \beta_{M,N},
  \]
  where \(\beta_{M,N}\) is the tensor-of-\(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\) reconciliation
  (\cref{def:pullback_val_iso}, \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}) on the \(\delta\)-codomain,
  identifying the \(a_Y\)-image of the presheaf tensor with \(f^{*}M \otimes_Y f^{*}N\).
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{lem:pullback_tensor_map, def:pullback_val_iso}
  This is the definitional unfolding of \(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\) (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map}):
  the sheaf comparison was constructed precisely as the sheafified presheaf cotensorator, pre- and
  post-composed with the \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\) reconciliations on source and target. The stated
  equation is that construction read back.
nodes.lean_source:
private lemma pullbackTensorMap_eq_sheafify_delta {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) (M N : X.Modules) :
    letI φ' : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
        (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
        (f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom
    pullbackTensorMap f M N
      = (SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom.app
            (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val)
          ≫ (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
              (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ') M.val N.val)
          ≫ (sheafifyTensorUnitIso (X := Y)
              ((PresheafOfModules.pullback φ').obj M.val)
              ((PresheafOfModules.pullback φ').obj N.val)).hom
          ≫ (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
              (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
                (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
                ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackValIso f M).hom)
                ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackValIso f N).hom)) :=
  rfl

nodes.statement:
ok
[Cone A sub-lemma 1: \texttt{pullbackValIso} naturality at the sheaf left unitor (RHS reconciliation)]
  
  
  
  Let \(f : Y \to X\) and \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\); write
  \(F = \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi'\) for the presheaf-level change-of-rings pullback, \(a_Y\) for the
  sheafification functor on \(Y\), and \(\mathtt{pbv}_{(-)} = \mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,(-)\)
  (\cref{def:pullback_val_iso}). The reconciliation isomorphism \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\) is
  natural; instantiated against the sheaf-level left unitor
  \((\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\,M).\mathrm{hom} : \mathcal{O}_X \otimes M \to M\), its naturality
  square identifies the \(f^{*}\)-image of that unitor --- the right-hand side of the identity of
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_left_unitality} --- as the \(a_Y\)-sheafification of the
  \(F\)-image of the unitor's underlying presheaf morphism, conjugated by \(\mathtt{pbv}\) on source
  and target:
  \[
    (\mathtt{pbv}_{\mathcal{O}_X \otimes M}).\mathrm{hom}
      \mathbin{;} f^{*}\bigl((\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\,M).\mathrm{hom}\bigr)
    = a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl(F.\mathrm{map}\,(\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\,M).\mathrm{hom}_{\mathrm{val}}\bigr)
      \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{pbv}_{M}).\mathrm{hom},
  \]
  where \((-)_{\mathrm{val}}\) denotes the underlying presheaf morphism (the forget-image) of a sheaf
  morphism.
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{def:pullback_val_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
  Object-wise, \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\) is the inverse \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\)
  comparison post-composed with the \(f^{*}\)-image of the sheafification counit
  (\cref{def:pullback_val_iso}); both legs are natural transformations. Hence the displayed equation
  is precisely the naturality of \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\) against the sheaf morphism
  \((\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\,M).\mathrm{hom} : \mathcal{O}_X \otimes M \to M\). Unfolding the
  sheaf-level unitor as the sheafified presheaf unitor \(a_Y.\mathrm{map}\,\lambda\) followed by the
  counit (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}) and cancelling that counit against the counit leg inside
  \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\) reduces the square to the naturality of the
  \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\) comparison, which is structural.
nodes.lean_source:
private lemma pullbackValIso_naturality_leftUnitor {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) (M : X.Modules) :
    (pullbackValIso f (tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) M)).hom
        ≫ (pullback f).map (tensorObj_left_unitor M).hom
      = (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
          ((PresheafOfModules.pullback (f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom).map
            ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).map (tensorObj_left_unitor M).hom))
        ≫ (pullbackValIso f M).hom :=
  pullbackValIso_naturality f (tensorObj_left_unitor M).hom

nodes.title: Localization-monoidal left-unitor collapse
nodes.statement:
\textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  Let \(L : \mathcal{C} \to \mathcal{D}\) be a localization functor for a monoidal morphism property
  \(W\), so that \(\mathcal{D} = \mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\,L\,W\,\varepsilon\) carries the transported
  (Gabriel--Zisman / Day-style) monoidal structure, with unit comparison \(\varepsilon\) and tensor
  comparison \(\mu_{X,Y} : L X \otimes L Y \cong L(X \otimes Y)\). For every object \(Y\) of
  \(\mathcal{C}\), the localized left unitor at the image object \(L Y\) collapses to the \(L\)-image of
  the source left unitor, modulo the unit and tensor comparisons:
  \[
    (\lambda_{L Y}).\mathrm{hom}
      = \varepsilon^{-1} \rhd L Y
        \mathbin{;} (\mu_{\mathbf 1,\,Y}).\mathrm{hom}
        \mathbin{;} L.\mathrm{map}\,(\lambda_Y).\mathrm{hom},
  \]
  where \(\mathbf 1\) is the source tensor unit. The proof is definitional: the localized left unitor
  is constructed as a \(\mathtt{Localization.liftNatIso}\) of the source unitor, so on an image object it
  reduces by \cref{lem:localization_lift_nat_trans_app} to the source datum conjugated by the lifting
  isomorphisms, with no further coherence obligations.

nodes.title: Evaluation of a lifted natural transformation on an image object
nodes.statement:
\textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  Let \(L : \mathcal{C} \to \mathcal{D}\) be a localization functor for \(W\), let \(F_1, F_2 :
  \mathcal{C} \to \mathcal{E}\) be functors that lift to \(F_1', F_2' : \mathcal{D} \to \mathcal{E}\)
  along \(L\), and let \(\tau : F_1 \to F_2\). The unique lifted natural transformation
  \(\mathtt{liftNatTrans}\,\tau : F_1' \to F_2'\), evaluated at an image object \(L X\), is the source
  datum conjugated by the lifting isomorphisms:
  \[
    (\mathtt{liftNatTrans}\,\tau)_{L X}
      = (\mathtt{Lifting.iso}\,F_1')_{X}.\mathrm{hom}
        \mathbin{;} \tau_X
        \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{Lifting.iso}\,F_2')_{X}.\mathrm{inv}.
  \]
  Thus a coherence between lifted transformations, tested on objects in the image of \(L\), reduces
  definitionally to the corresponding coherence of the source data pinned by the lifting iso --- the
  mechanism underlying the localization-monoidal unitor collapse above.

nodes.title: The sheafification of presheaves of modules is a localization
nodes.statement:
\textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  Let \(\alpha : R_0 \to R.\mathrm{obj}\) be the comparison from a presheaf of rings to its
  sheafification (locally injective and locally surjective). The sheafification reflector
  \(a = \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}\,\alpha : \mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R_0 \to
  \mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,R\) is a localization functor for the morphism class
  \(W = J.W.\mathtt{inverseImage}\,(\mathtt{toPresheaf}\,R_0)\) of presheaf morphisms inverted by
  sheafification. Consequently the entire \(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal}\) API ---
  \cref{lem:localization_monoidal_left_unitor_hom_app} and
  \cref{lem:localization_lift_nat_trans_app} in particular --- is applicable to \(a\).

ok
[STU-collapse: the sheaf left unitor at a pullback image object]
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  Let \(f : Y \to X\), write \(a = a_Y = \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}\) for the
  sheafification reflector on \(Y\), \(\varepsilon = \mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}\) for its unit comparison
  and \(\mu = \mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\) for its tensor comparison, and \(F = \mathtt{pullback}\,
  \varphi'\) for the presheaf-level change-of-rings pullback. For the image object
  \(a.\mathrm{obj}\,(F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}})\) --- the sheafification of the presheaf pullback
  of \(M\) --- the sheaf-level left unitor collapses, modulo \(\varepsilon\) and \(\mu\), to the
  sheafified presheaf left unitor:
  \[
    \bigl(\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\,(a.\mathrm{obj}\,(F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}))\bigr).\mathrm{hom}
      = \varepsilon^{-1} \rhd a.\mathrm{obj}\,(F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}})
        \mathbin{;} (\mu_{\mathbf 1,\,F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}).\mathrm{hom}
        \mathbin{;} a.\mathrm{map}\,\bigl(\lambda_{F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}\bigr).
  \]
  This is the shared structural residual of \cref{lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_pullback_eq_sheafify} and
  of the central-leg sub-step of
  \cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso_whisker_eq_sheafify_eta_whisker}.
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{lem:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso_hom_eq_prime, def:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
  The proof is a by-hand presheaf reduction across the sheafification adjunction; it does \emph{not}
  use the localization-monoidal API. The genuine content is a tensor-bifunctoriality plus
  adjunction-triangle collapse over the \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,(\mathbf 1)\) / \(\mathbf 1_{\mathtt{Functor}}\)
  defeq seam, not a definitional pass-through.

  \emph{Step 1: single-tensor presentation.} Present
  \(\mu = \mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\) by its single-tensor form
  (\cref{lem:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso_hom_eq_prime}, \cref{def:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso}):
  \(\mu.\mathrm{hom} = a.\mathrm{map}(\eta_{\mathbf 1} \otimes \eta_P)\), where
  \(\eta = (\mathtt{sheafificationAdjunction}).\mathrm{unit}\) is the sheafification unit and \(a\) the
  reflector; and expand \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\) at the image object as the sheafified
  presheaf left unitor (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}). All factors are kept inside the one
  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) monoidal instance on the \(\circ\,\mathtt{forget}_2\) carrier.

  \emph{Step 2: presheaf collapse identity.} Reduce the goal to the pure presheaf equation
  \[
    (\eta_{\mathbf 1} \otimes \eta_P) \mathbin{;} (\varepsilon \otimes \mathbf 1) \mathbin{;} \lambda_{aP}
      = \lambda_P \mathbin{;} \eta_P ,
  \]
  obtained from tensor bifunctoriality (the interchange law
  \((f_1\otimes f_2)\circ(g_1\otimes g_2) = (f_1\circ g_1)\otimes(f_2\circ g_2)\)), the
  sheafification-adjunction \emph{right-triangle} \(\eta_{\mathbf 1}\mathbin{;}\varepsilon = \mathbf 1\)
  on the unit factor, and presheaf left-unitor naturality of \(\eta_P\).

  \emph{Step 3: merge the functor-image legs and cancel the counit.} Both sides are
  \(a.\mathrm{map}\)-images; merge them by functoriality \(F(g)\circ F(h)=F(g\circ h)\), apply the
  Step-2 identity inside \(a.\mathrm{map}\), and cancel the trailing counit by the sheafification
  adjunction \emph{left-triangle}. This leaves exactly the displayed equation. The merge, the
  triangle rewrites, and the unit-factor whisker are all forced to be term-mode
  (\(\mathtt{congrArg}_2\)/\(\mathtt{Eq.trans}\)) because the \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,(\mathbf 1)\)
  wrapper on \(\eta\)'s codomain makes the connecting objects defeq-but-not-syntactic, so
  \(\mathtt{rw}\)/\(\mathtt{erw}\) miss or \(\mathtt{whnf}\)-bomb.
nodes.lean_source:
private lemma tensorObj_left_unitor_image_collapse {Y : Scheme.{u}}
    (P : _root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
    (sheafifyTensorUnitIso (X := Y)
          (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) P).hom
        ≫ (tensorObjIsoOfIso (sheafifyUnitIso (Y := Y))
            (Iso.refl ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
              (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj P))).hom
        ≫ (tensorObj_left_unitor ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
            (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj P)).hom
      = (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
          ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := Y.presheaf)).leftUnitor P).hom := by
  rw [sheafifyTensorUnitIso_hom_eq', tensorObjIsoOfIso_hom, tensorObj_functoriality,
    tensorObj_left_unitor]
  simp only [Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom, asIso_hom, Iso.app_hom]

  have hpre :
      (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
          (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
          ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
            (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).unit.app
            (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
          ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
            (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).unit.app P)
        ≫ MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
            (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
            ((sheafifyUnitIso (Y := Y)).hom.val)
            ((Iso.refl ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
              (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj P)).hom.val))
        ≫ ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := Y.presheaf)).leftUnitor
            ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
              (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj P).val).hom
      = ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := Y.presheaf)).leftUnitor P).hom
        ≫ (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
            (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).unit.app P := by
    have htri : (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
            (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).unit.app
            (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
          ≫ (sheafifyUnitIso (Y := Y)).hom.val
        = 𝟙 _ := by
      rw [sheafifyUnitIso]

      have h := (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
          (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).right_triangle_components (SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf)
      simp only [Iso.app_hom, asIso_hom] at h ⊢
      exact h
    simp only [Iso.refl_hom, SheafOfModules.id_val]
    rw [MonoidalCategory.tensorHom_comp_tensorHom, ← MonoidalCategory.leftUnitor_naturality]
    congr 1
    exact congrArg₂ (fun a b => MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) a b)
      htri (Category.comp_id _)
  erw [← Functor.map_comp_assoc, ← Functor.map_comp_assoc]

  refine Eq.trans (congrArg (fun m => (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
      (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map m ≫ (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
      (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit.app
      ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj P)) hpre) ?_
  beta_reduce
  erw [Functor.map_comp_assoc]

  have htr := (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
    (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).left_triangle_components P
  exact (congrArg (fun z => (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
    (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct
    (R := Y.presheaf)).leftUnitor P).hom ≫ z) htr).trans (Category.comp_id _)

nodes.statement:
ok
[Cone A sub-lemma 2: the pullback-object left unitor is the sheafified presheaf unitor]
  
  
  
  With notation as above, write \(\mathtt{pbv}_{(-)} = \mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,(-)\) and let
  \[
    W_{\lambda}
      = (\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}_{\mathbf 1,\,F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}).\mathrm{hom}
        \mathbin{;} a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso} \otimes (\mathtt{pbv}\,M)\bigr)
  \]
  be the \emph{unit-reconciled} \((\mathbf 1,\,f^{*}M)\)-wrapper: its tensor unit is the presheaf unit
  \(\mathbf 1 = \mathbf 1_{\mathtt{PSh}}\) and its left tensor leg is the unit comparison
  \(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}\) (\emph{not} the \(\delta\)-wrapper \(W\) of
  \cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso_whisker_eq_sheafify_eta_whisker}, whose unit is \(F\,\mathbf 1\) and whose
  left leg is \(\mathtt{pbv}\,\mathcal{O}_X\)). Post-composed with the sheaf-level left unitor at the
  pullback object \(f^{*}M\), \(W_{\lambda}\) equals the sheafified presheaf left unitor of
  \(F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\), conjugated by \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\) on the target:
  \[
    (\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}_{\mathbf 1,\,F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}).\mathrm{hom}
      \mathbin{;} a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso} \otimes (\mathtt{pbv}\,M)\bigr)
      \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\,(f^{*}M)).\mathrm{hom}
    = a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl(\lambda_{F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}\bigr)
      \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{pbv}_{M}).\mathrm{hom},
  \]
  where \(\mathbf 1 = \mathbf 1_{\mathtt{PSh}}\) is the presheaf tensor unit. The unit-reconciled
  wrapper \(W_{\lambda}\) is precisely the right-hand tail produced by the
  \(\delta\)-side \(\eta\)-whisker identity
  \cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso_whisker_eq_sheafify_eta_whisker}; that lemma performs the
  \(W \to W_{\lambda}\) handoff that converts the \(\delta\)-wrapper into the unit-reconciled wrapper
  consumed here.
nodes.proof_tex:
\uses{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_naturality, lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_image_collapse, def:pullback_val_iso}
  Split the wrapper's tensor leg \(a_Y.\mathrm{map}(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso} \otimes (\mathtt{pbv}\,M))\)
  by functoriality of the sheaf tensor into the unit-factor whisker
  \(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso} \rhd (-)\) followed by the right-factor whisker
  \((-) \lhd (\mathtt{pbv}\,M)\). Naturality of the sheaf-level left unitor against the
  \(\mathtt{pbv}\,M\)-leg (\cref{lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_naturality}) slides that right-factor whisker
  past the unitor, producing the trailing \((\mathtt{pbv}_{M}).\mathrm{hom}\) on the right-hand side and
  reducing the unitor to its value at the image object
  \(a_Y.\mathrm{obj}\,(F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}})\). The residual --- the
  \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\) reconciliation \(\mu\) and the \(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}\)
  whisker \(\varepsilon\) composed against the sheaf left unitor at that image object --- is exactly the
  localization-image collapse \cref{lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_image_collapse}: the sheaf left unitor at
  the image object collapses, modulo \(\varepsilon\) and \(\mu\), to
  \(a_Y.\mathrm{map}(\lambda_{F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}})\). Cancelling the
  \(\varepsilon\)-whisker against the collapse's \(\varepsilon^{-1}\)-whisker leaves the displayed
  identity. No new sheaf-level monoidal structure is introduced --- only unitor naturality and the
  image-collapse \cref{lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_image_collapse}, whose own proof is the by-hand
  bifunctoriality + adjunction-triangle reduction recorded there.
nodes.lean_source:
private lemma tensorObj_left_unitor_pullback_eq_sheafify {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
    (M : X.Modules) :
    letI φ' : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
        (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
        (f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom
    (sheafifyTensorUnitIso (X := Y)
          (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
          ((PresheafOfModules.pullback φ').obj M.val)).hom
        ≫ (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
            (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
              (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
              ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (sheafifyUnitIso (Y := Y)).hom)
              ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackValIso f M).hom))
        ≫ (tensorObj_left_unitor ((pullback f).obj M)).hom
      = (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
          ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := Y.presheaf)).leftUnitor
            ((PresheafOfModules.pullback (f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom).obj M.val)).hom
        ≫ (pullbackValIso f M).hom := by

  have hw : (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
        (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
          (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
          ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (sheafifyUnitIso (Y := Y)).hom)
          ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackValIso f M).hom))
      = (tensorObjIsoOfIso (sheafifyUnitIso (Y := Y)) (pullbackValIso f M)).hom := rfl

  have hnat : (tensorObjIsoOfIso (Iso.refl (SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf))
            (pullbackValIso f M)).hom
        ≫ (tensorObj_left_unitor ((pullback f).obj M)).hom
      = (tensorObj_left_unitor ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
            (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
            ((PresheafOfModules.pullback (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom).obj M.val))).hom
          ≫ (pullbackValIso f M).hom := by
    have h := tensorObj_left_unitor_naturality (W := Y) (pullbackValIso f M)
    rw [← Iso.trans_hom, h, Iso.trans_hom]

  have hcomb : (tensorObjIsoOfIso (sheafifyUnitIso (Y := Y)) (pullbackValIso f M)).hom
        ≫ (tensorObj_left_unitor ((pullback f).obj M)).hom
      = (tensorObjIsoOfIso (sheafifyUnitIso (Y := Y))
            (Iso.refl ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
              (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
              ((PresheafOfModules.pullback (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom).obj M.val)))).hom
        ≫ (tensorObj_left_unitor ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
            (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
            ((PresheafOfModules.pullback (Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom).obj M.val))).hom
        ≫ (pullbackValIso f M).hom := by
    rw [tensorObjIsoOfIso_eq_comp, Iso.trans_hom, Category.assoc]

    exact congrArg (_ ≫ ·) hnat
  erw [hw, hcomb]

  have h := congrArg (· ≫ (pullbackValIso f M).hom)
    (tensorObj_left_unitor_image_collapse (Y := Y)
      ((PresheafOfModules.pullback (f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom).obj M.val))
  simp only [Category.assoc] at h ⊢
  exact h

set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in

nodes.statement:
ok
[Cone A sub-lemma 3: the whiskered unit comparison is the sheafified \(\eta\)-whisker]
  
  
  
  With notation as above, write \(\mathtt{pbv}_{(-)} = \mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,(-)\),
  \(\eta\,F = \mathtt{Functor.OplaxMonoidal.}\eta\,(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi')\) for the presheaf
  oplax unit (\cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}), and let
  \begin{align*}
    W &= (\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}_{F.\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf 1,\,F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}).\mathrm{hom}
      \mathbin{;} a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl((\mathtt{pbv}\,\mathcal{O}_X)\otimes(\mathtt{pbv}\,M)\bigr),\\
    W_{\lambda} &= (\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}_{\mathbf 1,\,F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}).\mathrm{hom}
      \mathbin{;} a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}\otimes(\mathtt{pbv}\,M)\bigr)
  \end{align*}
  be respectively the \(\delta\)-identification right wrapper of
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_eq_sheafify_delta} (tensor unit \(F\,\mathbf 1\), left leg
  \(\mathtt{pbv}\,\mathcal{O}_X\)) and the unit-reconciled wrapper of
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_pullback_eq_sheafify} (tensor unit \(\mathbf 1 =
  \mathbf 1_{\mathtt{PSh}}\), left leg \(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}\)). Then the wrapper \(W\),
  post-composed with the unit comparison whiskered into the left tensor factor, equals the sheafified
  presheaf \(\eta\)-whisker followed by the unit-reconciled wrapper \(W_{\lambda}\):
  \[
    W \mathbin{;}
    (\mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\,(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f)\,\mathbf 1_{f^{*}M}).\mathrm{hom}
    = a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl(\eta\,F \rhd F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\bigr)
      \mathbin{;} W_{\lambda}.
  \]
